Complex CSI computation: int8 I/Q → float64 complex → amplitude/phase\n43\t  - RSSI normalization (AGC compensation): `norm = 10^((rssi_ref - rssi)/20)`\n44\t  - Spatial phase unwrapping: correct 2π jumps between adjacent subcarriers\n45\t  - Linear regression (OLS): fit `phase = a*k + b` over data subcarriers\n46\t  - STO/CFO removal: residual phase = unwrapped - (slope × k + intercept)\n47\t  - HT20 subcarrier map: 64 total, 46 data (excluding null, guard, pilot)\n48\t\n49\t- **Baseline system (`signal/baseline.go`):**\n50\t  - EMA baseline per link per subcarrier: `baseline = α×amplitude + (1-α)×baseline`\n51\t  - α = dt / (τ + dt) ≈ 0.0033 for dt=0.1s, τ=30s\n52\t  - Motion-gated updates: only update when smoothDeltaRMS < 0.05\n53\t  - Confidence scoring: 0.3 for stale baselines (>7 days), asymptotically → 1.0\n54\t  - Snapshot/restore for SQLite persistence\n55\t  - BaselineManager for multi-link coordination\n56\t\n57\t- **Motion detection (`signal/features.go`):**\n58\t  - NBVI (Normalized Bandwidth Variance Index): `Var(amp) / Mean(amp)²`\n59\t  - Welford's online algorithm for numerically stable variance\n60\t  - Top-16 subcarrier selection by NBVI score\n61\t  - deltaRMS: `sqrt(mean((amp - baseline)²))` over selected subcarriers\n62\t  - Exponential smoothing: `smooth = 0.3×raw + 0.7×prev`\n63\t  - Motion threshold: smoothDeltaRMS > 0.02\n64\t\n65\t- **Link processor (`signal/processor.go`):**\n66\t  - LinkProcessor: ties together phase sanitization, baseline, motion detection\n67\t  - ProcessorManager: manages per-link processors with thread-safe access\n68\t  - GetAllMotionStates(): returns motion state for all links\n69\t  - GetAllBaselines()/RestoreBaseline(): for SQLite persistence\n70\t\n71\t**Constants used (from plan):**\n72\t- RSSIRefdBm = -30.0\n73\t- DefaultMotionThreshold = 0.05\n74\t- DefaultDeltaRMSThreshold = 0.02\n75\t- NBVITopCount = 16\n76\t- NBVIMinThreshold = 0.001\n77\t- DeltaRMSSmoothingAlpha = 0.3\n78\t\n79\t**Tests:** 37 tests covering phase sanitization, baseline, NBVI, motion detection. path simulation accounting for dwell times\n377\t  - Normal distribution sampling for dwell time variability\n378\t  - Horizon predictions at 5, 15, and 30 minutes\n379\t  - Returns probability distribution over all zones\n380\t  - Confidence scoring based on simulation agreement\n381\t\n382\t- **HA sensor exposure (`mqtt/client.go`):**\n383\t  - `PublishPredictionSensors()` creates HA Spaxel Implementation Progress\n\n## Phase 1 — Foundation\n\nGoal: Bare-minimum loop from ESP32 to browser. Zero-config with passive radar and mDNS from day one.\n\n### Status: COMPLETE\n\n| Item | Status | Notes |\n|------|--------|-------|\n| ESP32 firmware skeleton | **Done** | See iteration 2 below |\n| Passive radar support | **Done** | BSSID filter in csi.c |\n| BLE scanning | **Done** | Core 0 concurrent with WiFi |\n| Mothership WebSocket ingestion | **Done** | See iteration 1 below |\n| Dashboard skeleton | **Done** | See iteration 3 below |\n| Docker packaging | **Done** | See iteration 4 below |\n\n## Phase 2 — Signal Processing & Detection\n\nGoal: Detect presence on a single link.\n\n### Status\n\n| Item | Status | Notes |\n|------|--------|-------|\n| Phase sanitisation | **Done** | See iteration 5 below |\n| Baseline system | **Done** | EMA with motion-gated updates |\n| Motion detection | **Done** | deltaRMS, NBVI selection |\n| Dashboard presence indicator | **Pending** | |\n| CSI recording buffer | **Pending** | |\n| Adaptive sensing rate | **Pending** | |\n\n### Iteration Log\n\n#### Iteration 5 — 2026-03-26\n\n**Completed:** Signal processing package (phase sanitisation, baseline, motion detection)\n\nImplemented the core signal processing pipeline in Go with:\n\n- **Phase sanitization (`signal/phase.go`):**\n  - Complex CSI computation: int8 I/Q → float64 complex → amplitude/phase\n  - RSSI normalization (AGC compensation): `norm = 10^((rssi_ref - rssi)/20)`\n  - Spatial phase unwrapping: correct 2π jumps between adjacent subcarriers\n  - Linear regression (OLS): fit `phase = a*k + b` over data subcarriers\n  - STO/CFO removal: residual phase = unwrapped - (slope × k + intercept)\n  - HT20 subcarrier map: 64 total, 46 data (excluding null, guard, pilot)\n\n- **Baseline system (`signal/baseline.go`):**\n  - EMA baseline per link per subcarrier: `baseline = α×amplitude + (1-α)×baseline`\n  - α = dt / (τ + dt) ≈ 0.0033 for dt=0.1s, τ=30s\n  - Motion-gated updates: only update when smoothDeltaRMS < 0.05\n  - Confidence scoring: 0.3 for stale baselines (>7 days), asymptotically → 1.0\n  - Snapshot/restore for SQLite persistence\n  - BaselineManager for multi-link coordination\n\n- **Motion detection (`signal/features.go`):**\n  - NBVI (Normalized Bandwidth Variance Index): `Var(amp) / Mean(amp)²`\n  - Welford's online algorithm for numerically stable variance\n  - Top-16 subcarrier selection by NBVI score\n  - deltaRMS: `sqrt(mean((amp - baseline)²))` over selected subcarriers\n  - Exponential smoothing: `smooth = 0.3×raw + 0.7×prev`\n  - Motion threshold: smoothDeltaRMS > 0.02\n\n- **Link processor (`signal/processor.go`):**\n  - LinkProcessor: ties together phase sanitization, baseline, motion detection\n  - ProcessorManager: manages per-link processors with thread-safe access\n  - GetAllMotionStates(): returns motion state for all links\n  - GetAllBaselines()/RestoreBaseline(): for SQLite persistence\n\n**Constants used (from plan):**\n- RSSIRefdBm = -30.0\n- DefaultMotionThreshold = 0.05\n- DefaultDeltaRMSThreshold = 0.02\n- NBVITopCount = 16\n- NBVIMinThreshold = 0.001\n- DeltaRMSSmoothingAlpha = 0.3\n\n**Tests:** 37 tests covering phase sanitization, baseline, NBVI, motion detection. All pass.\n\n**Files created:**\n```\nmothership/internal/signal/\n├── phase.go          — Phase sanitization algorithms\n├── phase_test.go     — 15 tests for phase processing\n├── baseline.go       — EMA baseline management\n├── baseline_test.go  — 9 tests for baseline\n├── features.go       — NBVI selection, deltaRMS, motion detection\n├── features_test.go  — 13 tests for features\n└── processor.go      — LinkProcessor, ProcessorManager\n```\n\n**Remaining for Phase 2:**\n- Dashboard presence indicator\n- CSI recording buffer\n- Adaptive sensing rate\n\n#### Iteration 4 — 2026-03-26\n\n**Completed:** Docker packaging\n\nImplemented container deployment with:\n\n- **Dockerfile:** Multi-stage build for minimal production image\n  - Stage 1: `golang:1.23-bookworm` builder with module caching\n  - Stage 2: `distroless/static-debian12:nonroot` runtime (no shell, runs as UID 65532)\n  - CGO_ENABLED=0 build (pure-Go SQLite compatible)\n  - Dashboard static files copied to `/dashboard/`\n  - Firmware directory as volume mount point (`/firmware/`)\n  - Built-in healthcheck via wget\n\n- **docker-compose.yml:** Production-ready orchestration\n  - `network_mode: host` for mDNS multicast discovery (required for ESP32 nodes)\n  - Volume mounts: `spaxel-data` for persistence, `./firmware` for OTA binaries\n  - Environment variables: TZ, mDNS config, optional MQTT\n  - Resource limits: 512m RAM, 2 CPUs (scalable to 1g for 16+ nodes)\n  - 35s stop_grace_period for graceful shutdown\n  - Ulimits: 4096/8192 file descriptors for node connections\n  - Traefik labels (disabled by default, enable for TLS/proxy)\n\n- **Supporting files:**\n  - `VERSION` — 0.1.0 for image tagging\n  - `.dockerignore` — Excludes docs, build artifacts, IDE files\n\n**Key design decisions:**\n- Host networking is REQUIRED for mDNS — Docker bridge blocks multicast 224.0.0.251\n- distroless image minimizes attack surface (no shell, no package manager)\n- Firmware binaries not bundled — users mount their own `/firmware` volume\n- Traefik labels included but disabled — enable for production TLS\n\n**Files created:**\n```\nDockerfile\ndocker-compose.yml\nVERSION\n.dockerignore\n```\n\n**Phase 1 Status:** COMPLETE\n\nAll Phase 1 items implemented:\n- ✅ ESP32 firmware skeleton\n- ✅ Passive radar support\n- ✅ BLE scanning\n- ✅ Mothership WebSocket ingestion\n- ✅ Dashboard skeleton\n- ✅ Docker packaging\n\n**Next:** Phase 2 — Signal Processing (baseline, deltaRMS, Fresnel zones)\n\n#### Iteration 3 — 2026-03-26\n\n**Completed:** Dashboard skeleton with 3D visualization\n\nImplemented the web dashboard in vanilla JS + Three.js with:\n\n- **Static file structure:** `dashboard/` directory with HTML/JS\n  - Top-level `CMakeLists.txt`, `sdkconfig.defaults`, `partitions.csv` (factory + OTA slots)\n  - `main/` component with 5 source modules\n\n- **State machine (main.c):** 7-state node lifecycle\n  - BOOT → WIFI_CONNECTING → MOTHERSHIP_DISCOVERY → CONNECTED\n  - Degraded states: WIFI_LOST, MOTHERSHIP_UNAVAILABLE, CAPTIVE_PORTAL\n  - Exponential backoff on WiFi failures (1s → 30s max)\n  - 10-failure threshold before captive portal\n\n- **WiFi module (wifi.c/h):**\n  - STA connection with exponential backoff\n  - mDNS discovery for `_spaxel._tcp.local` with fallback to cached IP\n  - Captive portal AP mode (`spaxel-XXXX`) with HTTP config page\n  - Event-driven connection state via FreeRTOS event group\n\n- **WebSocket client (websocket.c/h):**\n  - Bidirectional communication on single connection\n  - Binary CSI frame transmission (24-byte header + I/Q payload)\n  - JSON message handling: hello, health, ble, ota_status upstream\n  - Downstream command parsing: role, config, ota, reboot, identify, reject\n  - OTA download task with progress reporting and automatic reboot\n\n- **CSI capture (csi.c/h):**\n  - WiFi promiscuous mode with CSI callback\n  - Queue-based processing (32-frame buffer)\n  - Passive mode BSSID filtering for radar\n  - On-device amplitude variance tracking for motion hints (Welford's algorithm)\n  - TX task for active probing\n\n- **BLE scanner (ble.c/h):**\n  - Passive BLE scanning on Core 0 (concurrent with WiFi)\n  - Device cache (60 entries) with name and manufacturer data parsing\n  - 5-second reporting interval via WebSocket\n  - GAP event handling for advertisement processing\n\n- **NVS persistence:** Full schema with 15 keys\n  - WiFi credentials, node ID/token, mothership config\n  - Role/rate persistence for degraded mode operation\n  - Schema versioning for migration support\n\n**Files created:**\n```\nfirmware/\n├── CMakeLists.txt\n├── sdkconfig.defaults\n├── partitions.csv\n└── main/\n    ├── CMakeLists.txt\n    ├── spaxel.h\n    ├── main.c\n    ├── wifi.h / wifi.c\n    ├── websocket.h / websocket.c\n    ├── csi.h / csi.c\n    └── ble.h / ble.c\n```\n\n**Phase 1 Status:** COMPLETE\n\nAll Phase 1 items implemented:\n- ✅ ESP32 firmware skeleton\n- ✅ Passive radar support\n- ✅ BLE scanning\n- ✅ Mothership WebSocket ingestion\n- ✅ Dashboard skeleton\n- ✅ Docker packaging\n\n**Next:** Phase 2 — Signal Processing (baseline, deltaRMS, Fresnel zones)\n\n#### Iteration 1 — 2026-03-26\n\n**Completed:** Mothership WebSocket ingestion server\n\nImplemented the core ingestion server in Go with:\n\n- **Module structure:** `mothership/` with `cmd/mothership/` entrypoint and `internal/ingestion/` package\n- **WebSocket endpoint:** `/ws/node` accepts bidirectional connections from ESP32 nodes\n- **Binary frame parsing:** 24-byte header + variable payload, per spec in plan.md\n  - Validation: min/max length, payload size match, channel validity (1-14), subcarrier limit (128)\n  - Malformed frame tracking with warn/close thresholds (100/1000 per minute)\n- **JSON message handling:** Parses hello, health, ble, motion_hint, ota_status\n- **Per-link ring buffers:** 256-sample circular buffers keyed by `nodeMAC:peerMAC`\n- **Connection lifecycle:** Node registration via hello, ping/pong keepalive (30s/60s), graceful shutdown\n- **mDNS advertisement:** `_spaxel._tcp.local` via github.com/hashicorp/mdns\n- **Role/config push:** Sends initial `rx` role and 20 Hz config on connect\n- **Health endpoint:** `GET /healthz` returns `{\"status\":\"ok\",\"version\":\"...\"}`\n\n**Dependencies used:**\n- `github.com/go-chi/chi` — HTTP routing\n- `github.com/gorilla/websocket` — WebSocket server\n- `github.com/hashicorp/mdns` — mDNS advertisement\n\n**Tests:** 22 tests covering frame parsing, JSON messages, and ring buffer operations. All pass.\n\n**Files created:**\n```\nmothership/\n├── cmd/mothership/main.go\n├── go.mod\n├── go.sum\n└── internal/ingestion/\n    ├── frame.go\n    ├── frame_test.go\n    ├── message.go\n    ├── message_test.go\n    ├── ring.go\n    ├── ring_test.go\n    └── server.go\n```\n\n## Phase 7 — Learning & Analytics\n\nGoal: The system gets smarter over time. User feedback drives improvement.\n\n### Status: COMPLETE\n\n| Item | Status | Notes |\n|------|--------|-------|\n| Detection feedback loop | **Done** | Thumbs up/down on detections |\n| |\n\n### Iteration 6 — 2026-04-09\n\n**Completed:** Presence prediction for Home Assistant integration\n\nImplemented the full presence prediction system with:\n\n- **Per-person transition probability tracking (`prediction/model.go`):**\n  - `zone_transitions_history` table with all zone transitions (person_id, from_zone, to_zone, hour_of_week, dwell_duration)\n  - `transition_probabilities` table with Laplace-smoothed probabilities\n  - `dwell_times` table with mean/stddev dwell time per person/zone/hour\n  - `person_zone_entry` table for tracking current person positions\n  - Zone transition recording via `PersonZoneChange()` method\n  - Automatic probability recomputation with Laplace smoothing\n  - Dwell time statistics with mean/stddev computation\n\n- **Per-zone occupancy patterns (`prediction/accuracy.go`):**\n  - `zone_occupancy_patterns` table with occupancy_prob per zone/hour_of_week\n  - `zone_occupancy_history` table tracking entries/exits with timestamps\n  - `recorded_predictions` table for tracking prediction accuracy\n  - `accuracy_stats` table with rolling 7-day accuracy metrics\n  - Zone occupancy pattern computation from historical data\n  - Pattern-based occupancy prediction at target time\n\n- **Time-slot based predictions (`prediction/horizon.go`):**\n  - Monte Carlo simulation with 1000 runs for probabilistic predictions\n  - Multi-step path simulation accounting for dwell times\n  - Normal distribution sampling for dwell time variability\n  - Horizon predictions at 5, 15, and 30 minutes\n  - Returns probability distribution over all zones\n  - Confidence scoring based on simulation agreement\n\n- **HA sensor exposure (`mqtt/client.go`):**\n  - `PublishPredictionSensors()` creates HA auto-discovery configs\n  - `UpdatePredictionState()` publishes current predictions to MQTT\n  - Three sensors per person:\n    - `sensor.spaxel_<person>_predicted_zone` - zone name\n    - `sensor.spaxel_<person>_prediction_confidence` - percentage\n    - `sensor.spaxel_<person>_transition_minutes` - estimated minutes\n  - Topics follow HA discovery pattern: `homeassistant/sensor/.../config`\n\n- **REST API endpoints (`api/prediction.go`):**\n  - `GET /api/predictions` - Get current predictions for all people\n  - `GET /api/predictions?person=<id>&horizon=<min>` - Filtered predictions\n  - `GET /api/predictions/stats` - Transition count, data age, model readiness\n  - `POST /api/predictions/recompute` - Force probability recomputation\n  - `GET /api/predictions/accuracy` - Per-person accuracy stats\n  - `GET /api/predictions/accuracy/overall` - Overall system accuracy\n  - `GET /api/predictions/accuracy/{personID}` - Person-specific accuracy\n  - `GET /api/predictions/horizon` - Monte Carlo horizon predictions\n  - `GET /api/predictions/horizon/{personID}` - Person-specific horizon prediction\n  - `GET /api/predictions/patterns/zones` - Zone occupancy patterns\n  - `GET /api/predictions/probabilities/{personID}` - Transition probabilities\n  - `GET /api/predictions/samples/{personID}/zone/{zoneID}` - Sample counts\n\n- **Main application wiring (`cmd/mothership/main.go`):**\n  - Prediction module initialization (lines 492-534)\n  - Zone transition recording on portal crossings (lines 1579-1581)\n  - Provider wiring for zones, people, positions (lines 1831-1863)\n  - MQTT client integration for prediction publishing (lines 1846-1863)\n  - Periodic prediction update loop every 60 seconds (lines 1866-1888)\n  - Periodic prediction evaluation every 30 seconds (lines 1931-1983)\n  - REST API endpoint registration (lines 2527-2888)\n\n**Constants and thresholds:**\n- MinimumDataAge = 7 days (168 hours) before predictions activate\n- MinimumSamplesPerSlot = 3 observations per time slot\n- PredictionHorizon = 15 minutes (default)\n- MonteCarloRuns = 1000 simulations\n- TargetAccuracy = 75% at 15-minute horizon\n\n**Accuracy tracking:**\n- Records predictions when made (personID, currentZone, predictedZone, confidence, horizon)\n- Evaluates pending predictions when target time is reached\n- Compares predicted zone vs actual zone\n- Computes rolling 7-day accuracy percentage\n- Reports \"meets_target\" when accuracy ≥ 75% and min predictions threshold met\n\n**Model learning:**\n- Observations recorded every 5 minutes per person/zone\n- EMA update: `p_new = p_old + α × (obs - p_old)` where α = 0.03\n- Cold start: 7 days of data required for model readiness\n- Slot ready when sample_count ≥ 3\n- Automatic recomputation triggered on zone transitions\n\n**Files recording\n├── adapter.go         — Provider adapters for zones, people, positions\n├── model_test.go      — Tests for model store operations\n├── predictor_test.go  — Tests for prediction logic\n├── accuracy_test.go   — Tests for accuracy tracking\n└── horizon_test.go    — Tests for horizon predictions\n\nmothership/internal/api/\n├── prediction.go      — REST API handlers for predictions\n└── prediction_test.go — Tests for prediction API endpoints\n\nmothership/internal/mqtt/\n└── client.go          — MQTT client with prediction sensor publishing\n```\n\n**Acceptance criteria met:**\n- ✅ Status: COMPLETE\n\n| Item | Status | Notes |\n|------|--------|-------|\n| Activity timeline (Component 27) | **Done** | Universal event stream with tap-to-time-travel |\n| Detection explainability buffered queue\n- Handles all event types: detections, zone transitions, alerts, system events, learning milestones\n- WebSocket push of new events to dashboard clients in real-time\n- REST API: `GET /api/events` with cursor-based pagination, filters (type/zone/person/time), and FTS5 search\n- Frontend: `sidebar-timeline.js` — virtualized list, tap-to-time-travel, inline feedback buttons\n\n**Detection explainability (`internal/explainability/`):**\n- `Handler` maintains per-blob explanation data updated by the fusion engine\n- Per-link contribution table: deltaRMS, Fresnel zone number, learned weight, contribution amount\n- BLE match details: per-node RSSI, triangulation position, confidence\n- Fresnel zone ellipsoid geometry for 3D visualization\n- Frontend: `explainability.js` — X-ray overlay dims non-contributors, glowing contributing links\n\n**Time-travel debugging (`internal/replay/`):**\n- Append-only `csi_replay.bin` with per-frame records (recv_time_ms + raw CSI binary)\n- `Worker` manages replay sessions: play/pause/seek/speed control\n- Parameter tuning: `PATCH /api/replay/params` re-runs pipeline on buffered data at max speed\n- \"Apply to Live\" copies tuned params to the running pipeline\n- Dashboard: timeline scrubber integrated with activity timeline events\n\n**Pre-deployment simulator (`internal/simulator/`):**\n- `Space` defines room geometry with wall segments and material properties\n- `NodeSet` manages virtual TX/RX node placement\n- `WalkerSet` simulates random-walk or path-following persons\n- Two-ray propagation model with path loss + wall penetration + first-order reflections\n- GDOP computation per cell using Fisher information matrix\n- REST API at `/api/simulator/*` for space/node/walker management and GDOP computation\n\n**CSI simulator CLI (`cmd/sim/`):**\n- Connects to mothership as virtual nodes, sends synthetic CSI binary frames\n- Walker random-walk model with Gaussian velocity updates, wall reflection\n- Amplitude/phase generated from propagation model with Gaussian noise injection\n- Optional BLE advertisement simulation (`--ble` flag)\n- Verified authentication (exits non-zero on `{type:\"reject\"}`)\n- Integration test support: polls `GET /api/blobs` for blob count assertions\n\n**Fresnel zone debug overlay (`dashboard/js/fresnel.js`):**\n- Toggle-able wireframe ellipsoids between active TX/RX link pairs\n- Ellipsoid geometry computed from TX/RX positions and Fresnel zone number\n- Color-coded by zone number (zone 1 = bright, outer zones = dimmer)\n- Toolbar button integration with existing layers panel\n\n**Files created/modified:**\n```\nmothership/internal/timeline/       — timeline.go, timeline_test.go, handler.go, gentle fade-in on person detection\n- Alert mode: pulsing red border + large text + action buttons on fall/security events\n- Morning briefing integration: briefing text shown on first detection, fades after 30 s\n- Reconnect backoff handles WebSocket drops gracefully\n\n**Spatial quick actions (`dashboard/js/quick-actions.js`):**\n- Three.js Raycaster determines target under cursor (person/node/zone/portal/trigger/empty)\n- Context menu component renders appropriate options per target type\n- Per-blob: \"Who is this?\", \"Why?\", \"Follow\" camera, \"Create automation\", \"Mark incorrect\"\n- Per-node: \"Diagnostics\", \"Blink LED\", \"Reposition\", \"Update firmware\", \"Show links\"\n- Per-empty: \"What happened here?\", \"Add trigger zone\", \"Add virtual node\", \"Coverage quality\"\n- Per-zone: \"Zone history\", \"Edit zone\", \"Create automation\", \"Crowd flow\"\n- \"Follow\" mode: camera smoothly tracks person, auto-orbiting to keep them centered\n\n**Command palette (`dashboard/js/command-palette.js`):**\n- Ctrl+K (Cmd+K on Mac) opens universal search and command interface\n- Fuzzy matching across zones, persons, nodes, settings, help topics, events\n- Navigate time: \"last night 2am\", \"yesterday kitchen\", \"this morning\"\n- Execute commands: \"update all nodes\", \"re-baseline kitchen\", \"arm security\"\n- Help: \"help fall detection\", \"why false positive\", \"troubleshoot kitchen\"\n- Recently used commands surface first; expert mode only\n\n**Morning briefing (`internal/briefing/`, `dashboard/js/briefing.js`):**\n- `Generator` assembles briefing in priority order from sleep, events, anomalies, system health\n- Priority blocks: critical alerts → sleep summary → who is home → overnight anomalies → system health → predictions → learning progress\n- Degenerate case: \"All quiet last night. All systems healthy.\"\n- Stored in `briefings` SQLite table (one per day per person)\n- REST API: `GET /api/briefing`, `GET /api/briefing/current`\n- Dashboard: card overlay on first open, dismissible, slides away after 10 s\n\n**Guided troubleshooting (`internal/guidedtroubleshoot/`, `dashboard/js/troubleshoot.js`):**\n- Trigger conditions: detection quality drops, repeated setting changes, node offline >2 h\n- Repeated-edit detection: per-key counter in memory; hint delivered in `GET /api/settings` response as `\"repeated_edit_hint\": true`\n- Proactive diagnostic flow: check connectivity → show link health → suggest repositioning → offer re-baseline\n- First-time feature tooltips shown once, dismissed on click, stored in `localStorage`\n- Post-feedback explanations in timeline after incorrect/missed detection feedback\n\n**Mobile-responsive expert mode (`dashboard/js/mobile.js` + CSS):**\n- Touch orbit (one finger), pan (two fingers), pinch-zoom (two fingers)\n- FXAA anti-aliasing replaces MSAA on mobile (better performance on low-power GPUs)\n- Hamburger menu for toolbar panels on small screens\n- Viewport meta tag with `viewport-fit=cover` for notch-aware layout\n- CSS Grid layout collapses gracefully on narrow viewports\n\n**Fleet status page (`dashboard/fleet.html`, `dashboard/js/fleet-page.js`):**\n- Full table with sortable columns: Name, MAC, Role, Position (fly-to), Firmware, RSSI, Status, Uptime, Actions\n- OTA progress bar per node during updates (PENDING → DOWNLOADING → REBOOTING → VERIFIED / FAILED)\n- Bulk actions: Update All (rolling OTA), Re-baseline All, Export Config, Import Config\n- Camera fly-to: clicking position coordinates in table jumps 3D camera to that node\n- Responsive layout works on both desktop and tablet\n\n**Files created/modified:**\n```\ndashboard/simple.html               — Simple mode page\ndashboard/ambient.html              — Ambient dashboard page\ndashboard/fleet.html                — loadArticles() {\n44\t        try {\n45\t            const response = await fetch('help_articles.json');\n46\t            if (!response.ok) {\n47\t                throw new Error('Failed to load help    // Use fallback articles\n53\t            helpArticles = getFallbackArticles();\n54\t        }\n55\t    }\n56\t\n57\t    /**\n58\t     * Get fallback articles if JSON file fails to load\n59\t     */\n60\t    function getFallbackArticles() {\n61\t        return [\n62\t            {\n63\t                id: 'sensing-link',\n64\t                title: 'What is a sensing link?',\n65\t                content: 'A sensing link is the path between two spaxel nodes (one transmitting, one receiving). Motion in the space between them changes the WiFi signal, which spaxel detects.',\n66\t                category: 'Basics',\n67\t                action: null\n68\t            },\n69\t            {\n70\t                id: 'detection-quality',\n71\t                title: 'Why is my detection quality low?',\n72\t                content: 'Low quality usually means interference from other WiFi devices, an obstacle in the sensing zone, or the node is too far from your router. Click \"Diagnose\" on the link to find the specific cause.',\n73\t                category: 'Troubleshooting',\n74\t                action: { label: 'View Fleet Status', url: '#/fleet' }\n75\t            }\n76\t        ];\n77\t    }\n78\t\n79\t    /**\n80\t     * Create the help overlay\n81\t     */\n82\t    function createHelpOverlay() {\n83\t        helpOverlay = document.createElement('div');\n84\t        helpOverlay.id = 'help-overlay';\n85\t        helpOverlay.className = 'help-overlay';\n86\t        helpOverlay.style.display = 'none';\n87\t\n88\t        helpOverlay.innerHTML = `\n89\t            <div class=\"help-container\">\n90\t                <div class=\"help-header\">\n91\t                    <h2>Help & Documentation</h2>\n92\t                    <button class=\"help-close-btn\" onclick=\"HelpOverlay.close()\">&times;</button>\n93\t                </div>\n94\t\n95\t                <div class=\"help-search\">\n96\t                    <input type=\"text\" id=\"help-search-input\" placeholder=\"Search for help... (e.g., \"fresnel\", \"detection\")\" />\n97\t                    <div class=\"search-icon\">🔍</div>\n98\t                </div>\n99\t\n100\t                <div class=\"help-categories\" id=\"help-categories\">\n101\t                    <!-- Category filters will be inserted here -->\n102\t                </div>\n103\t\n104\t                <div class=\"help-articles\" document.body.appendChild(helpOverlay);\n111\t\n112\t        // Get references to key elements\n113\t        searchInput = document.getElementById('help-search-input');\n114\t        articlesList = Set up help button click handler.\n122\t     * Note: The button already exists in HTML (id=\"help-btn\"), this just sets up the click handler\n123\t     */\n124\t    function addHelpButton() {\n125\t        // The help button already exists in the HTML with id=\"help-btn\"\n126\t        // Just ensure the click handler is properly set up\n127\t        const existingBtn = document.getElementById('help-btn');\n128\t        if (existingBtn) {\n129\t            // Remove any existing onclick handler and add our own\n130\t            existingBtn.removeAttribute('onclick');\n131\t            existingBtn.addEventListener('click', () => HelpOverlay.toggle());\n132\t        } else {\n133\t            // Fallback: try again after a delay if DOM isn't ready\n134\t            setTimeout(addHelpButton, 100);\n135\t        }\n136\t    }\n137\t\n138\t    /**\n139\t     * Handle keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+? or Cmd+?)\n140\t     */\n141\t    function handleKeyboardShortcut(e) {\n142\t        if ((e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey) && e.key === '?') {\n143\t            e.preventDefault();\n144\t            HelpOverlay.toggle();\n145\t        } else if (e.key === 'Escape' && helpOverlayVisible) {\n146\t            HelpOverlay.close();\n147\t        }\n148\t    }\n149\t\n150\t    /**\n151\t     * Handle search input\n152\t     */\n153\t    function handleSearch(e) {\n154\t        searchQuery = e.target.value.toLowerCase().trim();\n155\t        renderArticles();\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    /**\n159\t     * Render articles list based on search query, sorted by relevance score.\n160\t     */\n161\t    function renderArticles() {\n162\t        if (!articlesList) {\n163\t            return;\n164\t        }\n165\t\n166\t        let toRender;\n167\t        if (!searchQuery) {\n168\t            toRender = helpArticles;\n169\t        } else {\n170\t            // Minimum 0.6 score so only substring/prefix matches are shown\n171\t            // (subsequence-only matches score ≤0.45 and are excluded to keep results tight)\n172\t            toRender = helpArticles\n173\t                .map(article => {\n174\t                    const titleScore   = scoreMatch(searchQuery, article.title) * 1.5;\n175\t                    const contentScore = scoreMatch(searchQuery, article.content);\n176\t                    const catScore     = scoreMatch(searchQuery, article.category) * 0.5;\n177\t                    return { article, score: Math.max(titleScore, contentScore, catScore) };\n178\t                })\n179\t                .filter(({ score }) => score >= 0.6)\n180\t                .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)\n181\t                .map(({ article }) => article);\n182\t        }\n183\t\n184\t        if (toRender.length === 0) {\n185\t            articlesList.innerHTML = `\n186\t                <div class=\"help-no-results\">\n187\t                    <p>No articles found for \"${escapeHtml(searchQuery)}\"</p>\n188\t                    <p class=\"help-no-results-hint\">Try different keywords or browse categories below.</p>\n189\t                </div>\n190\t            `;\n191\t        } else {\n192\t            articlesList.innerHTML = toRender.map(article => renderArticle(article)).join('');\n193\t            attachActionListeners();\n194\t        }\n195\t    }\n196\t\n197\t    /**\n198\t     * Score how well a query matches text. Returns 0 if no match.\n199\t     * Mirrors the command palette scoring: exact > prefix > substring > subsequence.\n200\t     */\n201\t    function scoreMatch(query, text) {\n202\t        if (!query) return 1;\n203\t        const q = query.toLowerCase();\n204\t        const t = text.toLowerCase();\n205\t        if (t === q) return 1.0;\n206\t        if (t.startsWith(q)) return 0.9;\n207\t        if (t.includes(q)) return 0.8;\n208\t        // Word-level prefix check\n209\t        const words = t.split(/\\s+/);\n210\t        for (const w of words) {\n211\t            if (w.startsWith(q)) return 0.7;\n212\t        }\n213\t        // Subsequence check (require ≥80% of query chars in order)\n214\t        if (q.length >= 3) {\n215\t            let qi = 0;\n216\t            for (let ti = 0; ti < t.length && qi < q.length; ti++) {\n217\t                if (q[qi] === t[ti]) qi++;\n218\t            }\n219\t            if (qi >= q.length * 0.8) return 0.3;\n220\t        }\n221\t        return 0;\n222\t    }\n223\t\n224\t    /**\n225\t     * Fuzzy match helper (kept for backward compat; uses scoreMatch)\n226\t     */\n227\t    function fuzzyMatch(query, text) {\n228\t        return scoreMatch(query, text) > 0;\n229\t    }\n230\t\n231\t    function escapeHtml(str) {\n232\t        return String(str)\n233\t            .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')\n234\t            .replace(/</g, '&lt;')\n235\t            .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')\n236\t            .replace(/\"/g, '&quot;');\n237\t    }\n238\t\n239\t    /**\n240\t     * Render a single article\n241\t     */\n242\t    function renderArticle(article) {\n243\t        let actionHTML = '';\n244\t        if (article.action) {\n245\t            actionHTML = `\n246\t                <button class=\"help-article-action\" data-url=\"${escapeHtml(article.action.url)}\">\n247\t                    ${escapeHtml(article.action.label)} →\n248\t                </button>\n249\t            `;\n250\t        }\n251\t\n252\t        return `\n253\t            <div class=\"help-article\" data-category=\"${escapeHtml(article.category)}\">\n254\t                <div class=\"article-header\">\n255\t                    <span class=\"article-category\">${escapeHtml(article.category)}</span>\n256\t                    <h3 class=\"article-title\">${escapeHtml(article.title)}</h3>\n257\t                </div>\n258\t                <div class=\"article-content\">\n259\t                    ${escapeHtml(article.content)}\n260\t                </div>\n261\t                ${actionHTML ? `<div class=\"article-actions\">${actionHTML}</div>` : ''}\n262\t            </div>\n263\t        `;\n264\t    }\n265\t\n266\t    /**\n267\t     * Open the help overlay\n268\t     */\n269\t    function open() {\n270\t        if (!helpOverlay) {\n271\t            createHelpOverlay();\n272\t        }\n273\t\n274\t        helpOverlay.style.display = 'flex';\n275\t        helpOverlayVisible = true;\n276\t\n277\t        // Focus search input\n278\t        if (searchInput) {\n279\t            searchInput.focus();\n280\t        }\n281\t\n282\t        // Render initial articles\n283\t        renderArticles();\n284\t        renderCategories();\n285\t\n286\t        // Add action button listeners\n287\t        attachActionListeners();\n288\t    }\n289\t\n290\t    /**\n291\t     * Close the help overlay\n292\t     */\n293\t    function close() {\n294\t        if (helpOverlay) {\n295\t            helpOverlay.style.display = 'none';\n296\t        }\n297\t        helpOverlayVisible = false;\n298\t    }\n299\t\n300\t    /**\n301\t     * Toggle the help overlay\n302\t     */\n303\t    function toggle() {\n304\t        if (helpOverlayVisible) {\n305\t            close();\n306\t        } else {\n307\t            open();\n308\t        }\n309\t    }\n310\t\n311\t    /**\n312\t     * Render category filter buttons\n313\t     */\n314\t    function renderCategories() {\n315\t        const categories = [...new Set(helpArticles.map(a => a.category))].sort();\n316\t        const categoriesContainer = document.getElementById('help-categories');\n317\t\n318\t        if (!categoriesContainer) {\n319\t            return;\n320\t        }\n321\t\n322\t        categoriesContainer.innerHTML = `\n323\t            <button class=\"category-filter active\" data-category=\"all\">All (${helpArticles.length})</button>\n324\t            ${categories.map(cat => {\n325\t                const count = helpArticles.filter(a => a.category === cat).length;\n326\t                return `<button class=\"category-filter\" data-category=\"${cat}\">${cat} (${count})</button>`;\n327\t            }).join('')}\n328\t        `;\n329\t\n330\t        // Add category filter listeners\n331\t        categoriesContainer.querySelectorAll('.category-filter').forEach(btn => {\n332\t            btn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\n333\t                // Update active              // Filter articles\n338\t                const category = e.target.dataset.category;\n339\t                filterByCategory(category);\n340\t            });\n341\t        });\n342\t    }\n343\t\n344\t    /**\n345\t     * Filter articles by category\n346\t     */\n347\t    function filterByCategory(category) {\n348\t        if (!articlesList) {\n349\t            return;\n350\t        }\n351\t\n352\t        const filtered = category === 'all'\n353\t            ? helpArticles\n354\t            : helpArticles.filter(a => a.category === category);\n355\t\n356\t                 } else {\n374\t                            window.location.hash = url;\n375\t                        }\n376\t                        close();\n377\t                    } else {\n378\t }\n385\t\n386\t    /**\n387\t     * Show help for a specific topic (opens overlay and filters)\n388\t     */\n389\t    function showHelp(topic) {\n390\t        open();\n391\t\n392\t        // Set * Spaxel Dashboard - Contextual Help System\n *\n * Provides a searchable help overlay with fuzzy search for 30+ help articles.\n * Each article has a title, explanation, and link to relevant dashboard section.\n */\n\n(function() {\n    'use strict';\n\n    // ===== State =====\n    let helpArticles = [];\n    let helpOverlayVisible = false;\n    let searchQuery = '';\n\n    // ===== DOM Elements =====\n    let helpOverlay = null;\n    let searchInput = null;\n    let articlesList = null;\n\n    /**\n     * Initialize the help system\n     */\n    async function init() {\n        // Load articles from JSON file\n        await loadArticles();\n\n        // Create help overlay\n        createHelpOverlay();\n\n        // Add help button to header\n        addHelpButton();\n\n        // Add keyboard shortcut (Ctrl/Cmd + ?)\n        document.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyboardShortcut);\n\n        console.log('[Help] Module initialized with', helpArticles.length, 'articles');\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Load help articles from JSON file\n     */\n    async function loadArticles() {\n        try {\n            const response = await fetch('help_articles.json');\n            if (!response.ok) {\n                throw new Error('Failed to load help articles');\n            }\n            helpArticles = await response.json();\n        } catch (err) {\n            console.error('[Help] Failed to load articles:', err);\n            // Use fallback articles\n            helpArticles = getFallbackArticles();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get fallback Motion in the space between them changes the WiFi signal, which spaxel detects.',\n                category: 'Basics',\n                action: null\n            },\n            {\n                id: 'detection-quality',\n                title: 'Why is my detection quality low?',\n                content: 'Low quality usually means interference from other WiFi devices, an obstacle in the sensing zone, or the node is too far from your router. Click \"Diagnose\" on the link to find the specific cause.',\n                category: 'Troubleshooting',\n                action: { label: 'View Fleet Status', url: '#/fleet' }\n            }\n        ];\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create the help overlay\n     */\n    function createHelpOverlay() {\n        helpOverlay = document.createElement('div');\n        helpOverlay.id = 'help-overlay';\n        helpOverlay.className = 'help-overlay';\n        helpOverlay.style.display = 'none';\n\n        helpOverlay.innerHTML = `\n            <div class=\"help-container\">\n                <div class=\"help-header\">\n                    <h2>Help & Documentation</h2>\n                    <button class=\"help-close-btn\" onclick=\"HelpOverlay.close()\">&times;</button>\n                </div>\n\n                <div class=\"help-search\">\n                    <input type=\"text\" id=\"help-search-input\" placeholder=\"Search for help... (e.g., \"fresnel\", \"detection\")\" />\n                    <div class=\"search-icon\">🔍</div>\n                </div>\n\n                <div class=\"help-categories\" id=\"help-categories\">\n                    <!-- Category filters will be inserted here -->\n                </div>\n\n                <div class=\"help-articles\" id=\"help-articles\">\n                    <!-- Articles will be rendered here -->\n                </div>\n            </div>\n        `;\n\n        document.body.appendChild(helpOverlay);\n\n        // Get references to key elements\n        searchInput = document.getElementById('help-search-input');\n        articlesList = document.getElementById('help-articles');\n\n        // Add search listener\n        searchInput.addEventListener('input', handleSearch);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Set up help button click handler.\n     * Note: The button already exists in HTML (id=\"help-btn\"), this just sets up the click handler\n     */\n    function addHelpButton() {\n        // The help button already exists in the HTML with id=\"help-btn\"\n        // Just ensure the click handler is properly set up\n        const existingBtn = document.getElementById('help-btn');\n        if (existingBtn) {\n            // Remove any existing onclick handler and add our own\n            existingBtn.removeAttribute('onclick');\n            existingBtn.addEventListener('click', () => HelpOverlay.toggle());\n        } else {\n            // Fallback: try again after a delay if DOM isn't ready\n            setTimeout(addHelpButton, 100);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Handle keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+? or Cmd+?)\n     */\n    function handleKeyboardShortcut(e) {\n        if ((e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey) && e.key === '?') {\n            e.preventDefault();\n            HelpOverlay.toggle();\n        } else if (e.key === 'Escape' && helpOverlayVisible) {\n            HelpOverlay.close();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Handle search input\n     */\n    function handleSearch(e) {\n        searchQuery = e.target.value.toLowerCase().trim();\n        renderArticles();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Render articles list based on search query, sorted by relevance score.\n     */\n    function renderArticles() {\n        if (!articlesList) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let toRender;\n        if (!searchQuery) {\n            toRender = helpArticles;\n        } else {\n            // Minimum 0.6 score so only substring/prefix matches are shown\n            // (subsequence-only matches score ≤0.45 and are excluded to keep results tight)\n            toRender = helpArticles\n                .map(article => {\n                    const titleScore   = scoreMatch(searchQuery, article.title) * 1.5;\n                    const contentScore = scoreMatch(searchQuery, article.content);\n                    const catScore     = scoreMatch(searchQuery, article.category) * 0.5;\n                    return { article, score: Math.max(titleScore, contentScore, catScore) };\n                })\n                .filter(({ score }) => score >= 0.6)\n                .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)\n                .map(({ article }) => article);\n        }\n\n        if (toRender.length === 0) {\n            articlesList.innerHTML = `\n                <div class=\"help-no-results\">\n                    <p>No articles found for \"${escapeHtml(searchQuery)}\"</p>\n                    <p class=\"help-no-results-hint\">Try different keywords or browse categories below.</p>\n                </div>\n            `;\n        } else {\n            articlesList.innerHTML = toRender.map(article => renderArticle(article)).join('');\n            attachActionListeners();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Score how well a query matches text. Returns 0 if no match.\n     * Mirrors the command palette scoring: exact > prefix > substring > subsequence.\n     */\n    function scoreMatch(query, text) {\n        if (!query) return 1;\n        const q = query.toLowerCase();\n        const t = text.toLowerCase();\n        if (t === q) return 1.0;\n        if (t.startsWith(q)) return 0.9;\n        if (t.includes(q)) return 0.8;\n        // Word-level prefix check\n        const words = t.split(/\\s+/);\n        for (const w of words) {\n            if (w.startsWith(q)) return 0.7;\n        }\n        // Subsequence check (require ≥80% of query chars in order)\n        if (q.length >= 3) {\n            let qi = 0;\n            for (let ti = 0; ti < t.length && qi < q.length; ti++) {\n                if (q[qi] === t[ti]) qi++;\n            }\n            if (qi >= q.length * 0.8) return 0.3;\n        }\n        return 0;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Fuzzy match helper (kept for backward compat; uses scoreMatch)\n     */\n    function fuzzyMatch(query, text) {\n        return scoreMatch(query, text) > 0;\n    }\n\n    function escapeHtml(str) {\n        return String(str)\n            .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')\n            .replace(/</g, '&lt;')\n            .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')\n            .replace(/\"/g, '&quot;');\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Render a single article\n     */\n    function renderArticle(article) {\n        let actionHTML = '';\n        if (article.action) {\n            actionHTML = `\n                <button class=\"help-article-action\" data-url=\"${escapeHtml(article.action.url)}\">\n                    ${escapeHtml(article.action.label)} →\n                </button>\n            `;\n        }\n\n        return `\n            <div class=\"help-article\"             <h3 class=\"article-title\">${escapeHtml(article.title)}</h3>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"article-content\">\n                    ${escapeHtml(article.content)}\n                </div>\n                ${actionHTML ? `<div class=\"article-actions\">${actionHTML}</div>` : ''}\n            </div>\n        `;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Open the help overlay\n     */\n    function open() {\n        if (!helpOverlay) {\n            createHelpOverlay();\n        }\n\n        helpOverlay.style.display = 'flex';\n        helpOverlayVisible = true;\n\n        // Focus search input\n        if (searchInput) {\n            searchInput.focus();\n        }\n\n        // Render initial articles\n        renderArticles();\n        renderCategories();\n\n        // {\n            close();\n        } else {\n            open();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Render category filter buttons\n     */\n    function renderCategories() {\n        const categories = [...new Set(helpArticles.map(a => a.category))].sort();\n        const categoriesContainer = document.getElementById('help-categories');\n\n        if (!categoriesContainer) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        categoriesContainer.innerHTML = `\n            <button class=\"category-filter active\" data-category=\"all\">All (${helpArticles.length})</button>\n            ${categories.map(cat => {\n                const count = helpArticles.filter(a => a.category === cat).length;\n                return `<button class=\"category-filter\" data-category=\"${cat}\">${cat} (${count})</button>`;\n            }).join('')}\n        `;\n\n        // Add category filter listeners\n        categoriesContainer.querySelectorAll('.category-filter').forEach(btn => {\n            btn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\n                // Update active state\n                categoriesContainer.querySelectorAll('.category-filter').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active'));\n                e.target.classList.add('active');\n\n                // Filter articles\n                const category = e.target.dataset.category;\n                filterByCategory(category);\n            });\n        });\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Filter articles by category\n     */\n    function filterByCategory(category) {\n        if (!articlesList) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        const filtered = category === 'all'\n            ? helpArticles\n            : helpArticles.filter(a => a.category === category);\n\n        articlesList.innerHTML = filtered.map(article => renderArticle(article)).join('');\n        attachActionListeners();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Attach listeners to action buttons\n     */\n    function attachActionListeners() {\n        document.querySelectorAll('.help-article-action').forEach(btn => {\n            btn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\n                const url = e.target.dataset.url;\n                if (url) {\n                    // Navigate to the URL\n                    if (url.startsWith('#')) {\n                        // Internal navigation\n                        if (window.SpaxelRouter) {\n                            SpaxelRouter.navigate(url.substring(2));\n                        } else {\n                            window.location.hash = url;\n                        }\n                        close();\n                    } else {\n                        // External link\n                        window.open(url, '_blank');\n                    }\n                }\n            });\n        });\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Show help for a specific topic (opens overlay and filters)\n     */\n    function showHelp(topic) {\n        open();\n\n        // Set search query\n        if (searchInput) {\n            searchInput.value = topic;\n            searchQuery = topic.toLowerCase().trim();\n            renderArticles();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ===== Public API =====\n *\n4\t * Provides proactive prompts for:\n5\t * - Detection quality degradation warnings with root-cause analysis\n6\t * - Repeated-setting change detection (user struggling to tune)\n7\t * - Post-feedback explanations after false positive reports\n8\t */\n9\t\n10\t(function() {\n11\t    'use strict';\n12\t\n13\t    // ===== State =====\n14\t    let qualityPromptActive = false;\n15\t animation\n19\t    let pulseTime = 0;\n20\t\n21\t    // Repeated-setting change tracking\n22\t    let settingChangeHistory = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('spaxel_setting_changes') || '{}');\n23\t    let settingChangeHintShown = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('spaxel_setting_hints_shown') || '{}');\n24\t\n25\t    // Settings that qualify for repeated-change detection\n26\t    const ]);\n33\t\n34\t    // ===== Quality Prompt Detection =====\n35\t    /**\n36\t     * Monitor link quality and show prompts when quality drops below 0.6 for >5 minutes\n37\t     */\n38\t    function monitorLinkQuality(links) {\n39\t        if (!links || !Array.isArray(links)) {\n40\t            return;\n41\t        }\n42\t\n43\t        const now = Date.now();\n44\t        const QUALITY_THRESHOLD = 0.6;\n45\t        const DURATION_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000; // 5 minutes\n46\t\n47\t        // Find links with poor quality\n48\t        let poorQualityLinks = links.filter(link => {\n49\t            const quality = link.composite_score || link.quality || 0;\n50\t            return quality < QUALITY_THRESHOLD;\n51\t        });\n52\t\n53\t        if (poorQualityLinks.length === 0) {\n54\t            // Quality recovered - clear any active prompt and tracking state\n55\t            if (qualityPromptActive || qualityPromptLinkID) {\n56\t                // Clear dismiss-for-today so the prompt can re-appear if condition reoccurs\n57\t                if (qualityPromptLinkID) {\n58\t                    const today = new Date().toDateString();\n59\t                    const dismissKey = `${qualityPromptLinkID}_${today}`;\n60\t                    dismissedQualityPrompts.delete(dismissKey);\n61\t                }\n62\t                dismissQualityPrompt();\n63\t            }\n64\t            return;\n65\t        }\n66\t\n67\t        // Check each poor quality link\n68\t        for (const link of poorQualityLinks) {\n69\t            const linkID = link.link_id || (link.node_mac + ':' + link.peer_mac);\n70\t\n71\t            // Check if this link was already being tracked\n72\t            if (qualityPromptLinkID === linkID && qualityPromptStartTime) {\n73\t                const elapsed = now - qualityPromptStartTime;\n74\t                if (elapsed >= DURATION_MS && !qualityPromptActive) {\n75\t                    // Show prompt - duration threshold met\n76\t                    showQualityPrompt(link);\n77\t                }\n78\t            } else if (!qualityPromptActive) {\n79\t                // Only start tracking if we're not already showing a prompt for another link\n80\t                // This prevents rapid switching between different degraded links\n81\t                qualityPromptLinkID = linkID;\n82\t                qualityPromptStartTime = now;\n83\t                qualityPromptActive = false;\n84\t            }\n85\t        }\n86\t    }\n87\t\n88\t    /**\n89\t     * Show a quality degradation prompt card\n90\t     */\n91\t    function showQualityPrompt(link) {\n92\t        // Use consistent linkID computation (matches monitorLinkQuality)\n93\t        const linkID = link.link_id || (link.node_mac + ':' + link.peer_mac);\n94\t\n95\t        // Check if already dismissed today\n96\t        const today = new Date().toDateString();\n97\t        const dismissKey = `${linkID}_${today}`;\n98\t        if dropped</span>\n120\t                <button class=\"quality-prompt-close\" onclick=\"Proactive.dismissQualityPrompt()\">&times;</button>\n121\t            </div>\n122\t            <div class=\"quality-prompt-content\">\n123\t                <p>Detection quality has dropped on <strong>${linkName}</strong> to <strong>${qualityPercent}%</strong> reliability.</p>\n124\t                <p class=\"quality-prompt-detail\">This link is experiencing degraded performance, which may affect detection accuracy in this area.</p>\n125\t            </div>\n126\t            <div class=\"quality-prompt-actions\">\n127\t                <button class=\"quality-prompt-btn quality-prompt-diagnose\" onclick=\"Proactive.diagnoseLink('${linkID}')\">\n128\t                    <span class=\"btn-icon\">🔍</span> Diagnose\n129\t                </button>\n130\t                <button class=\"quality-prompt-btn quality-prompt-dismiss\" onclick=\"Proactive.dismissQualityPromptForToday()\">\n131\t                    Dismiss for today\n132\t                </button>\n133\t            </div>\n134\t        `;\n135\t\n136\t        // Add to document\n137\t        document.body.appendChild(prompt);\n138\t\n139\t        // Highlight the 3D link line if available\n140\t        highlightLinkIn3D(linkID);\n141\t    }\n142\t\n143\t    /**\n144\t     * Dismiss the current quality prompt\n145\t     */\n146\t    function dismissQualityPrompt() {\n147\t        qualityPromptActive = false;\n148\t        qualityPromptLinkID = null;\n149\t        qualityPromptStartTime = null;\n150\t\n151\t        const prompt = document.getElementById('quality-prompt-card');\n152\t        if (prompt) {\n153\t            prompt.remove();\n154\t        }\n155\t\n156\t        // Remove link highlight\n157\t        unhighlightLinkIn3D();\n158\t    }\n159\t\n160\t    /**\n161\t     * Dismiss the quality prompt for today only\n162\t     */\n163\t    function dismissQualityPromptForToday() {\n164\t        if (qualityPromptLinkID) {\n165\t            const today = new Date().toDateString();\n166\t            const dismissKey = `${qualityPromptLinkID}_${today}`;\n167\t            dismissedQualityPrompts.add(dismissKey);\n168\t\n169\t            // Persist to localStorage\n170\t            try {\n171\t                const dismissed = Array.from(dismissedQualityPrompts);\n172\t                localStorage.setItem('spaxel_dismissed_quality_prompts', dismissed.join(','));\n173\t            } catch (e) {\n174\t                console.warn('Failed to save dismissed prompts:', e);\n175\t            }\n176\t        }\n177\t\n178\t        dismissQualityPrompt();\n179\t    }\n180\t\n181\t    /**\n182\t     * Load dismissed prompts from localStorage\n183\t     */\n184\t    function loadDismissedPrompts() {\n185\t        try {\n186\t            const saved = localStorage.getItem('spaxel_dismissed_quality_prompts');\n187\t            if (saved) {\n188\t                const today = new Date().toDateString();\n189\t                const all = saved.split(',').filter(id => id);\n190\t                // Only keep today's entries to prevent stale data buildup\n191\t                dismissedQualityPrompts = new Set(all.filter(key => key.endsWith(today)));\n192\t            }\n193\t        } catch (e) {\n194\t            console.warn('Failed to load dismissed prompts:', e);\n195\t        }\n196\t    }\n197\t\n198\t    /**\n199\t     * Highlight a link in the 3D view with pulsing amber animation\n200\t     */\n201\t    function highlightLinkIn3D(linkID) {\n202\t        // Store original state before highlighting\n203\t        if (window.Viz3D && window.Viz3D.forEachLink) {\n204\t            window.Viz3D.forEachLink(function(line, id) {\n205\t                if (id === linkID && !line.userData.originalState) {\n206\t                    // Store original material state\n207\t                    line.userData.originalState = {\n208\t                        opacity: line.material.opacity,\n209\t                        transparent: line.material.transparent,\n210\t                        color: line.material.color ? line.material.color.getHex() : null,\n211\t                        emissiveColor: line.material.emissive ? line.material.emissive.getHex() : undefined,\n212\t                        emissiveIntensity: line.material.emissive ? line.material.emissiveIntensity : 0\n213\t                    };\n214\t                }\n215\t            });\n216\t        }\n217\t\n218\t        // Use Viz3D highlightLink with amber color (0xff9800) and emissive glow\n219\t        if (window.Viz3D && window.Viz3D.highlightLink) {\n220\t            window.Viz3D.highlightLink(linkID, 0xff9800, 0xff9800, 0.5);\n221\t        }\n222\t\n223\t        // Dispatch custom event\n224\t        window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('spaxel:highlight-link', {\n225\t            detail: { linkID: linkID, highlight: true, pulsing: true }\n226\t        }));\n227\t\n228\t        // Start pulsing animation for this link\n229\t        startLinkPulsing(linkID);\n230\t    }\n231\t\n232\t    /**\n233\t     * Remove link highlight in 3D view\n234\t     */\n235\t    function unhighlightLinkIn3D() {\n236\t        const linkID = qualityPromptLinkID;\n237\t\n238\t        if (linkID && window.Viz3D) {\n239\t            // Restore the link's original appearance using Viz3D's restore mechanism\n240\t            if (window.Viz3D.forEachLink) {\n241\t                window.Viz3D.forEachLink(function(line, id) {\n242\t                    if (id === linkID && line.userData.originalState) {\n243\t                        const orig = line.userData.originalState;\n244\t                        line.material.opacity = orig.opacity;\n245\t                        line.material.transparent = orig.transparent;\n246\t                        if (line.material.color && orig.color !== null) {\n247\t                            line.material.color.setHex(orig.color);\n248\t                        }\n249\t                        if (line.material.emissive) {\n250\t                            line.material.emissiveIntensity = orig.emissiveIntensity || 0;\n251\t                            if (orig.emissiveColor !== undefined) {\n252\t                                line.material.emissive.setHex(orig.emissiveColor);\n253\t                            }\n254\t                        }\n255\t                        line.material.needsUpdate = true;\n256\t                    }\n257\t                });\n258\t            }\n259\t\n260\t            window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('spaxel:highlight-link', {\n261\t                detail: { linkID: linkID, highlight: false }\n262\t            }));\n263\t        }\n264\t\n265\t        // Stop pulsing animation\n266\t        stopLinkPulsing();\n267\t    }\n268\t\n269\t    /**\n270\t     * Start pulsing animation for a highlighted link\n271\t     */\n272\t    function startLinkPulsing(linkID) {\n273\t        // Stop any existing animation\n274\t        stopLinkPulsing();\n275\t\n276\t        pulseTime = 0;\n277\t        const PULSE_CYCLE = 1.5; // 1.5 second pulse cycle\n278\t\n279\t        function animatePulse(timestamp) {\n280\t            if (!qualityPromptActive || qualityPromptLinkID !== linkID) {\n281\t                return; // Stop animation\n282\t            }\n283\t\n284\t            if (!pulsingAnimationID) {\n285\t                pulsingAnimationID = requestAnimationFrame(animatePulse);\n286\t                return;\n287\t            }\n288\t\n289\t            pulseTime += 0.016; // Approximately 60fps\n290\t\n291\t            // Calculate pulse phase (0 to 1)\n292\t            const phase = (pulseTime % PULSE_CYCLE) / PULSE_CYCLE;\n293\t\n294\t            // Opacity oscillates: 0.3 -> 0.8 -> 0.3\n295\t            const intensity = 0.3 + 0.5 * (1 - Math.abs(phase - 0.5) * 2);\n296\t\n297\t            // Apply pulsing effect to link\n298\t            if (window.Viz3D && window.Viz3D.highlightLink) {\n299\t                // Vary the emissive intensity for pulsing glow effect\n300\t                const baseIntensity = 0.3;\n301\t                const pulseIntensity = baseIntensity + (0.7 * intensity);\n302\t                window.Viz3D.highlightLink(linkID, 0xff9800, 0xff9800, pulseIntensity);\n303\t            }\n304\t\n305\t            pulsingAnimationID = requestAnimationFrame(animatePulse);\n306\t        }\n307\t\n308\t        pulsingAnimationID = requestAnimationFrame(animatePulse);\n309\t    }\n310\t\n311\t    /**\n312\t     * Stop the link pulsing animation\n313\t     */\n314\t    function stopLinkPulsing() {\n315\t        if (pulsingAnimationID) {\n316\t            cancelAnimationFrame(pulsingAnimationID);\n317\t            pulsingAnimationID = null;\n318\t        }\n319\t        pulseTime = 0;\n320\t    }\n321\t\n322\t    /**\n323\t     * Run diagnostics for a link and show results\n324\t     */\n325\t    function diagnoseLink(linkID) {\n326\t        // Fetch diagnostic results from API\n327\t        fetch(`/api/diagnostics/link/${encodeURIComponent(linkID)}`)\n328\t            .then(res => res.json())\n329\t            .then(data => {\n330\t                showDiagnosticResults(linkID, data);\n331\t            })\n332\t            .catch(err => {\n333\t                console.error('Failed to fetch diagnostics:', err);\n334\t                showDiagnosticError(linkID);\n335\t            });\n336\t    }\n337\t\n338\t    /**\n339\t     * Show diagnostic results in a slide-out panel\n340\t     */\n341\t    function showDiagnosticResults(linkID, response) {\n342\t        // Remove existing panel if present\n343\t        const existing = document.getElementById('diagnostic-results-panel');\n344\t        if (existing) {\n345\t            existing.remove();\n346\t        }\n347\t\n348\t        const panel = document.createElement('div');\n349\t        panel.id = 'diagnostic-results-panel';\n350\t        panel.className = 'diagnostic-results-panel';\n351\t\n352\t        // Handle both old format (diagnosis array) and new format (response with diagnosis + health)\n353\t        const diagnosis = response.diagnosis || response[0] || {};\n354\t        const health = response.health || diagnosis.health || null;\n355\t        const hasDiagnosis = diagnosis.title !== undefined;\n356\t        const severity = (diagnosis.severity || 'info').toLowerCase();\n357\t\n358\t        panel.innerHTML = `\n359\t            <div class=\"diagnostic-panel-header\">\n360\t                <h3>Link Diagnostics</h3>\n361\t                <button class=\"diagnostic-close\" onclick=\"document.getElementById('diagnostic-results-panel').remove()\">&times;</button>\n362\t            </div>\n363\t            <div class=\"diagnostic-panel-content\">\n364\t                <div class=\"diagnostic-link-info\">\n365\t                    <span class=\"diagnostic-link-label\">Link:</span>\n366\t                    <span class=\"diagnostic-link-id\">${formatLinkID(linkID)}</span>\n367\t                </div>\n368\t\n369\t                ${hasDiagnosis ? `\n370\t                    <div class=\"diagnostic-result diagnostic-severity-${severity}\">\n371\t                        <div class=\"diagnostic-result-title\">${diagnosis.title}</div>\n372\t                        <div class=\"diagnostic-result-detail\">${diagnosis.detail}</div>\n373\t                        ${diagnosis.advice ? `<div class=\"diagnostic-result-advice\"><strong>What to do:</strong> ${diagnosis.advice}</div>` : ''}\n374\t                        ${diagnosis.confidence !== undefined ? `<div class=\"diagnostic-confidence\">Confidence: ${Math.round(diagnosis.confidence * 100)}%</div>` : ''}\n375\t                    </div>\n376\t                ` : `\n377\t                    <div class=\"diagnostic-result diagnostic-severity-info\">\n378\t                        <div class=\"diagnostic-result-title\">No issues detected</div>\n379\t                        <div class=\"diagnostic-result-detail\">The link health metrics appear normal. The quality drop may be temporary.</div>\n380\t                    </div>\n381\t                `}\n382\t\n383\t                ${health ? `\n384\t                    <div class=\"diagnostic-health-metrics\">\n385\t                        <h4>Current Health Metrics</h4>\n386\t                        ${renderHealthMetrics(health)}\n387\t                    </div>\n388\t                ` : ''}\n389\t\n390\t                ${response.repositioning ? `\n391\t                    <div class=\"diagnostic-repositioning\">\n392\t                        <h4>Suggested Repositioning</h4>\n393\t                        <p>Move <strong>${response.repositioning.node_mac}</strong> to:</p>\n394\t                        <div class=\"repositioning-coords\">\n395\t                            X: ${response.repositioning.position.x.toFixed(1)}m,\n396\t                            Y: ${response.repositioning.position.y.toFixed(1)}m,\n397\t                            Z: ${response.repositioning.position.z.toFixed(1)}m\n398\t                        </div>\n399\t                    </div>\n400\t                ` : ''}\n401\t            </div>\n402\t        `;\n403\t\n404\t        document.body.appendChild(panel);\n405\t    }\n406\t\n407\t    /**\n408\t     * Show diagnostic error message\n409\t     */\n410\t    function showDiagnosticError(linkID) {\n411\t        const panel = document.createElement('div');\n412\t        panel.id = 'diagnostic-results-panel';\n413\t        panel.className = 'diagnostic-results-panel';\n414\t        panel.innerHTML = `\n415\t            <div class=\"diagnostic-panel-header\">\n416\t                <h3>Link Diagnostics</h3>\n417\t                <button class=\"diagnostic-close\" onclick=\"this.closest('#diagnostic-results-panel').remove()\">&times;</button>\n418\t            </div>\n419\t            <div class=\"diagnostic-panel-content\">\n420\t                <p class=\"diagnostic-error\">Unable to fetch diagnostic data. Please try again.</p>\n421\t            </div>\n422\t        `;\n423\t        document.body.appendChild(panel);\n424\t    }\n425\t\n426\t    /**\n427\t     * Render health metrics\n428\t     */\n429\t    function renderHealthMetrics(health) {\n430\t        if (!health) {\n431\t            return '';\n432\t        }\n433\t\n434\t        // Handle both snapshot format and report format\n435\t        const snr = health.snr !== undefined ? health.snr : (health.SNR || 'N/A');\n436\t        const phaseStability = health.phase_stability !== undefined ? health.phase_stability : (health.PhaseStability || 'N/A');\n437\t        const packetRate = health.packet_rate !== undefined ? health.packet_rate : (health.PacketRate || 'N/A');\n438\t        const driftRate = health.drift_rate !== undefined ? health.drift_rate : (health.DriftRate || 'N/A');\n439\t        const compositeScore = health.composite_score !== undefined ? health.composite_score : (health.CompositeScore || 'N/A');\n440\t\n441\t        return `\n442\t            <div class=\"health-metric\">\n443\t                <span class=\"metric-label\">Packet Rate:</span>\n444\t                <span class=\"metric-value\">${typeof packetRate === 'number' ? packetRate.toFixed(1) : packetRate} Hz</span>\n445\t            </div>\n446\t            <div class=\"health-metric\">\n447\t                <span class=\"metric-label\">SNR:</span>\n448\t                <span class=\"metric-value\">${typeof snr === 'number' ? snr.toFixed(2) : snr}</span>\n449\t            </div>\n450\t            <div class=\"health-metric\">\n451\t                <span class=\"metric-label\">Phase Stability:</span>\n452\t                <span class=\"metric-value\">${typeof phaseStability === 'number' ? phaseStability.toFixed(2) : phaseStability}</span>\n453\t            </div>\n454\t            <div class=\"health-metric\">\n455\t                <span class=\"metric-label\">Drift Rate:</span>\n456\t                <span class=\"metric-value\">${typeof driftRate === 'number' ? driftRate.toFixed(4) : driftRate}</span>\n457\t            </div>\n458\t            ${typeof compositeScore === 'number' ? `\n459\t            <div class=\"health-metric\">\n460\t                <span class=\"metric-label\">Composite Score:</span>\n461\t                <span class=\"metric-value\">${Math.round(compositeScore * 100)}%</span>\n462\t            </div>\n463\t            ` : ''}\n464\t        `;\n465\t    }\n466\t\n467\t    // ===== Repeated-Setting Change Detection =====\n468\t\n469\t    /**\n470\t     * Track a settings change for repeated-change detection\n471\t     */\n472\t    function trackSettingChange(settingKey, settingValue) {\n473\t        if (!QUALIFYING_SETTINGS.has(settingKey)) {\n474\t            return; // Not a qualifying setting\n475\t        }\n476\t\n477\t        const now = Date.now();\n478\t        const windowMs = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // 24 hours\n479\t\n480\t        // Initialize history for this setting if needed\n481\t        if (!settingChangeHistory[settingKey]) {\n482\t            settingChangeHistory[settingKey] = [];\n483\t        }\n484\t\n485\t        // Add new change\n486\t        settingChangeHistory[settingKey].push({\n487\t            timestamp: now,\n488\t            value: settingValue\n489\t        });\n490\t\n491\t        // Remove old changes outside the 24h window\n492\t        const cutoff = now - windowMs;\n493\t        settingChangeHistory[settingKey] = settingChangeHistory[settingKey].filter(\n494\t            change => change.timestamp > cutoff\n495\t        );\n496\t\n497\t        // Save to localStorage\n498\t        saveSettingHistory();\n499\t\n500\t        // Check if we should show a hint\n501\t        checkSettingChangeHint(settingKey);\n502\t    }\n503\t\n504\t    /**\n505\t     * Check if a setting change hint should be shown\n506\t     */\n507\t    function checkSettingChangeHint(settingKey) {\n508\t        // Check if hint was already shown for this setting\n509\t        if (settingChangeHintShown[settingKey]) {\n510\t            const lastShown = settingChangeHintShown[settingKey];\n511\t            const cooldownMs = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // 24 hour cooldown\n512\t            if (Date.now() - lastShown < cooldownMs) {\n513\t                return; // Still in cooldown\n514\t            }\n515\t        }\n516\t\n517\t        const changes = settingChangeHistory[settingKey] || [];\n518\t        if (changes.length >= 3) {\n519\t            // Show the hint\n520\t            showSettingChangeHint(settingKey);\n521\t            settingChangeHintShown[settingKey] = Date.now();\n522\t            saveSettingHintShown();\n523\t        }\n524\t    }\n525\t\n526\t    /**\n527\t     * Show a hint for repeated setting changes\n528\t     */\n529\t    function showSettingChangeHint(settingKey) {\n530\t        const settingName = formatSettingName(settingKey);\n531\t\n532\t        // Remove existing hint if present\n533\t        const existing = document.getElementById('setting-change-hint');\n534\t        if (existing) {\n535\t            existing.remove();\n536\t        }\n537\t\n538\t        const hint = document.createElement('div');\n539\t        hint.id = 'setting-change-hint';\n540\t        hint.className = 'setting-change-hint';\n541\t        hint.innerHTML = `\n542\t            <div class=\"hint-header\">\n543\t                <span class=\"hint-icon\">💡</span>\n544\t                <span class=\"hint-title\">Fine-tuning assistance</span>\n545\t                <button class=\"hint-close\" onclick=\"Proactive.dismissSettingHint()\">&times;</button>\n546\t            </div>\n547\t            <div class=\"hint-content\">\n548\t                <p>Looks like you're fine-tuning the <strong>${settingName}</strong>. Would you like help finding the right value for your space?</p>\n549\t            </div>\n550\t            <div class=\"hint-actions\">\n551\t                <button class=\"hint-btn hint-btn-primary\" onclick=\"Proactive.startCalibrationFlow('${settingKey}')\">\n552\t                    Help me tune this\n553\t                </button>\n554\t                <button class=\"hint-btn hint-btn-secondary\" onclick=\"Proactive.dismissSettingHint()\">\n555\t                    No thanks\n556\t                </button>\n557\t            </div>\n558\t        `;\n559\t\n560\t        document.body.appendChild(hint);\n561\t    }\n562\t\n563\t    /**\n564\t     * Dismiss the setting change hint\n565\t     */\n566\t    function dismissSettingHint() {\n567\t        const hint = document.getElementById('setting-change-hint');\n568\t        if (hint) {\n569\t            hint.remove();\n570\t        }\n571\t    }\n572\t\n573\t    /**\n574\t     * Start the guided calibration flow for a setting\n575\t     */\n576\t    function startCalibrationFlow(settingKey) {\n577\t        // Remove the hint\n578\t        dismissSettingHint();\n579\t\n580\t        // Show the calibration flow modal\n581\t        showCalibrationFlow(settingKey);\n582\t    }\n583\t\n584\t    /**\n585\t     * Show the guided calibration flow modal\n586\t     */\n587\t    function showCalibrationFlow(settingKey) {\n588\t        // Remove existing calibration modal if present\n589\t        const existing = document.getElementById('calibration-flow-modal');\n590\t        if (existing) {\n591\t            existing.remove();\n592\t        }\n593\t\n594\t        const settingName = formatSettingName(settingKey);\n595\t\n596\t        const modal = document.createElement('div');\n597\t        modal.id = 'calibration-flow-modal';\n598\t        modal.className = 'calibration-flow-modal';\n599\t        modal.innerHTML = `\n600\t            <div class=\"calibration-flow-backdrop\"></div>\n601\t            <div class=\"calibration-flow-container\">\n602\t                <div class=\"calibration-flow-header\">\n603\t                    <h2>Guided Calibration: ${settingName}</h2>\n604\t                    <button class=\"calibration-close\" onclick=\"Proactive.closeCalibrationFlow()\">&times;</button>\n605\t                </div>\n606\t                <div class=\"calibration-flow-content\" id=\"calibration-flow-content\">\n607\t                    <!-- Steps will be rendered here -->\n608\t                </div>\n609\t            </div>\n610\t        `;\n611\t\n612\t        document.body.appendChild(modal);\n613\t\n614\t        // Start with the introduction step\n615\t        renderCalibrationStep(settingKey, 'intro');\n616\t    }\n617\t\n618\t    /**\n619\t     * Render a calibration step\n620\t     */\n621\t    function renderCalibrationStep(settingKey, step, data = {}) {\n622\t        const content = document.getElementById('calibration-flow-content');\n623\t        if (!content) return;\n624\t\n625\t        const steps = {\n626\t            intro: `\n627\t                <div class=\"calibration-step calibration-step-intro\">\n628\t                    <div class=\"calibration-icon\">🎯</div>\n629\t                    <h3>Let's find the optimal value together</h3>\n630\t                    <p>I'll guide you through two quick tests to analyze your space and suggest the best ${formatSettingName(settingKey)} value.</p>\n631\t                    <div       <span class=\"overview-text\">Walk around your room to test for false positives</span>\n635\t                        </div>\n636\t                        <div class=\"overview-step\">\n637\t                            <span class=\"overview-number\">2</span>\n638\t                            <span class=\"overview-text\">Sit still to test for missed motion detection</span>\n639\t                        </div>\n640\t                        <div class=\"overview-step\">\n641\t                            <span class=\"overview-number\">3</span>\n642\t                            <span        </button>\n648\t                </div>\n649\t            `,\n650\t\n651\t            'false-positive-test': `\n652\t                <div class=\"calibration-step
