{\n170\t\tde.mu.Lock()\n171\t\tde.getOccupancyState = fn\n172\t\tde.mu.Unlock()\n173\t}\n174\t\n175\t// Run starts the diagnostic engine background loop\n176\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) Run(ctx context.Context) {\n177\t\tde.mu.Lock()\n178\t\tif de.running {\n179\t\t\tde.mu.Unlock()\n180\t\t\treturn\n181\t\t}\n182\t\tde.running = true\n183\t\tde.ctx, de.cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)\n184\t\tde.mu.Unlock()\n185\t\n186\t\tticker := time.NewTicker(de.config.DiagnosticInterval)\n187\t\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n188\t\n189\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] diagnostics: engine started (interval: %v)\", de.config.DiagnosticInterval)\n190\t\n191\t\tfor {\n192\t\t\tselect {\n193\t\t\tcase <-de.ctx.Done():\n194\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] diagnostics: engine stopped\")\n195\t\t\t\treturn\n196\t\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n197\t\t\t\tde.runDiagnosticPass()\n198\t\t\t}\n199\t\t}\n200\t}\n201\t\n202\t// Stop stops the diagnostic engine\n203\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) Stop() {\n204\t\tde.mu.Lock()\n205\t\tdefer de.mu.Unlock()\n206\t\tif de.cancel != nil {\n207\t\t\tde.cancel()\n208\t\t}\n209\t\tde.running = false\n210\t}\n211\t\n212\t// runDiagnosticPass runs all diagnostic rules on all links\n213\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) runDiagnosticPass() {\n214\t\tde.mu.RLock()\n215\t\tgetAllLinkIDs := de.getAllLinkIDs\n216\t\tde.mu.RUnlock()\n217\t\n218\t\tif getAllLinkIDs == nil {\n219\t\t\treturn\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\tlinkIDs := getAllLinkIDs()\n223\t\tfor _, linkID := range linkIDs {\n224\t\t\tdiagnoses := de.diagnoseLink(linkID)\n225\t\t\tif len(diagnoses) > 0 {\n226\t\t\t\tde.storeDiagnoses(linkID, diagnoses)\n227\t\t\t}\n228\t\t}\n229\t}\n230\t\n231\t// diagnoseLink runs all diagnostic rules on a single link\n232\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) diagnoseLink(linkID string) []Diagnosis {\n233\t\tde.mu.RLock()\n234\t\tgetHealthHistory := de.getHealthHistory\n235\t\twindow := de.config.HistoryWindow\n236\t\tde.mu.RUnlock()\n237\t\t_ = de.getFeedbackEvents\n238\t\t_ = de.getOccupancyState\n239\t\n240\t\tif getHealthHistory == nil {\n241\t\t\treturn nil\n242\t\t}\n243\t\n244\t\thistory := getHealthHistory(linkID, window)\n245\t\tif len(history) < de.config.MinSamples {\n246\t\t\treturn nil\n247\t\t}\n248\t\n249\t\tvar diagnoses []Diagnosis\n250\t\n251\t\t// Rule 1: Environmental Change\n252\t\tif d := de.checkEnvironmentalChange(linkID, history); d != nil {\n253\t\t\tdiagnoses = append(diagnoses, *d)\n254\t\t}\n255\t\n256\t\t// Rule 2: WiFi Congestion or Distance\n257\t\tif d := de.checkWiFiCongestion(linkID, history); d != nil {\n258\t\t\tdiagnoses = append(diagnoses, *d)\n259\t\t}\n260\t\n261\t\t// Rule 3: Near-Field Metal Interference\n262\t\tif d := de.checkMetalInterference(linkID, history); d != nil {\n263\t\t\tdiagnoses = append(diagnoses, *d)\n264\t\t}\n265\t\n266\t\t// Rule 4: Fresnel Zone Blockage\n267\t\tif d := de.checkFresnelBlockage(linkID, history); d != nil {\n268\t\t\tdiagnoses = append(diagnoses, *d)\n269\t\t}\n270\t\n271\t\t// Rule 5: Periodic Interference Spikes\n272\t\tif d := de.checkPeriodicInterference(linkID, history); d != nil {\n273\t\t\tdiagnoses = append(diagnoses, *d)\n274\t\t}\n275\t\n276\t\treturn diagnoses\n277\t}\n278\t\n279\t// Rule 1: Environmental Change\n280\t// Trigger: High baseline drift (>5% per hour) correlated across multiple links simultaneously (>50% of active links).\n281\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) checkEnvironmentalChange(linkID string, history []LinkHealthSnapshot) *Diagnosis {\n282\t\tde.mu.RLock()\n283\t\tgetAllLinkIDs := de.getAllLinkIDs\n284\t\tgetHealthHistory := de.getHealthHistory\n285\t\tde.mu.RUnlock()\n286\t\n287\t\tif getAllLinkIDs == nil || getHealthHistory == nil {\n288\t\t\treturn nil\n289\t\t}\n290\t\n291\t\t// Calculate drift rate for this link\n292\t\tavgDrift := calculateAverageDrift(history)\n293\t\tif avgDrift < 0.05 { // < 5% per hour\n294\t\t\treturn nil\n295\t\t}\n296\t\n297\t\t// Check if drift is correlated across multiple links\n298\t\tallLinks := getAllLinkIDs()\n299\t\tcorrelatedCount := 0\n300\t\ttotalActive := 0\n301\t\n302\t\tfor _, lid := range allLinks {\n303\t\t\tlhistory := getHealthHistory(lid, de.config.HistoryWindow)\n304\t\t\tif len(lhistory) < de.config.MinSamples {\n305\t\t\t\tcontinue\n306\t\t\t}\n307\t\t\ttotalActive++\n308\t\t\tlavgDrift := calculateAverageDrift(lhistory)\n309\t\t\tif lavgDrift >= 0.05 {\n310\t\t\t\tcorrelatedCount++\n311\t\t\t}\n312\t\t}\n313\t\n314\t\tif totalActive == 0 {\n315\t\t\treturn nil\n316\t\t}\n317\t\n318\t\tcorrelationRatio := float64(correlatedCount) / float64(totalActive)\n319\t\tif correlationRatio < 0.5 {\n320\t\t\treturn nil // Not correlated enough\n321\t\t}\n322\t\n323\t\t// Confidence: 0.85 if drift is correlated across >50% of links\n324\t\tconfidence := 0.85\n325\t\n326\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n327\t\t\tLinkID:           linkID,\n328\t\t\tRuleID:           \"environmental_change\",\n329\t\t\tSeverity:         SeverityINFO,\n330\t\t\tTitle:            \"Environmental change detected\",\n331\t\t\tDetail:           \"Multiple sensing links are showing simultaneous baseline shifts. This typically indicates a temperature change, or a large object was moved in the space. The system is adapting automatically.\",\n332\t\t\tAdvice:           \"No action needed. The baseline will re-stabilise within 30 minutes.\",\n333\t\t\tRepositioningTarget: nil,\n334\t\t\tConfidenceScore:  confidence,\n335\t\t\tTimestamp:        time.Now(),\n336\t\t}\n337\t}\n338\t\n339\t// Rule 2: WiFi Congestion or Distance\n340\t// Trigger: Packet rate health < 0.8 for more than 10 minutes on a single link.\n341\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) checkWiFiCongestion(linkID string, history []LinkHealthSnapshot) *Diagnosis {\n342\t\t// Check if packet rate has been low for > 10 minutes\n343\t\t// Expected rate is 20 Hz, so health < 0.8 means < 16 Hz\n344\t\tpacketHealthThreshold := 0.8\n345\t\tminDuration := 10 * time.Minute\n346\t\n347\t\tif len(history) < 2 {\n348\t\t\treturn nil\n349\t\t}\n350\t\n351\t\t// Find the time span of history (handles both ascending and descending order)\n352\t\tstartTime := history[0].Timestamp\n353\t\tendTime := history[len(history)-1].Timestamp\n354\t\tduration := endTime.Sub(startTime)\n355\t\tif duration < 0 {\n356\t\t\tduration = -duration\n357\t\t}\n358\t\n359\t\tif duration < minDuration {\n360\t\t\treturn nil\n361\t\t}\n362\t\n363\t\t// Check if packet rate health has been consistently low\n364\t\tlowCount := 0\n365\t\ttotalCount := 0\n366\t\tvar avgPacketRate float64\n367\t\n368\t\tfor _, h := range history {\n369\t\t\t// Packet rate health: actual/expected (20 Hz)\n370\t\t\trateHealth := h.PacketRate / 20.0\n371\t\t\tavgPacketRate += h.PacketRate\n372\t\t\ttotalCount++\n373\t\t\tif rateHealth < packetHealthThreshold {\n374\t\t\t\tlowCount++\n375\t\t\t}\n376\t\t}\n377\t\n378\t\tif totalCount == 0 {\n379\t\t\treturn nil\n380\t\t}\n381\t\n382\t\tavgPacketRate /= float64(totalCount)\n383\t\tlowRatio := float64(lowCount) / float64(totalCount)\n384\t\n385\t\t// Require > 80% of samples to have low packet rate\n386\t\tif lowRatio < 0.8 {\n387\t\t\treturn nil\n388\t\t}\n389\t\n390\t\t// Extract node B MAC from linkID\n391\t\tnodeBMAC := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n392\t\n393\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n394\t\t\tLinkID:           linkID,\n395\t\t\tRuleID:           \"wifi_congestion_distance\",\n396\t\t\tSeverity:         SeverityACTIONABLE,\n397\t\t\tTitle:            \"Node has low signal rate\",\n398\t\t\tDetail:           formatWiFiDetail(nodeBMAC, avgPacketRate),\n399\t\t\tAdvice: formatWiFiAdvice(nodeBMAC),\n400\t\t\tRepositioningTarget: nil,\n401\t\t\tRepositioningNodeMAC: nodeBMAC,\n402\t\t\tConfidenceScore:  0.75,\n403\t\t\tTimestamp:        time.Now(),\n404\t\t}\n405\t}\n406\t\n407\t// Rule 3: Near-Field Metal Interference\n408\t// Trigger: Low phase stability (< 0.4) sustained for > 30 minutes during known-quiet periods.\n409\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) checkMetalInterference(linkID string, history []LinkHealthSnapshot) *Diagnosis {\n410\t\t// Phase stability: lower is more stable. We need to check if stability score is low.\n411\t\t// In the codebase, PhaseStability is normalized variance where < 0.4 is stable\n412\t\t// So \"low phase stability\" means the value is HIGH (> 0.6 or so)\n413\t\t// Actually looking at ambient.go: stability score of 0.1 is very stable, 1.0 is unstable\n414\t\t// The task says \"Low phase stability (< 0.4)\" but this is confusing.\n415\t\t// Looking at weather.go: phaseStabilityWarn = 0.5, phaseStabilityCrit = 0.8\n416\t\t// I'll interpret \"low phase stability\" as \"high phase variance\" i.e. PhaseStability > 0.6\n417\t\n418\t\tphaseThreshold := 0.6 // High variance = unstable\n419\t\tminDuration := 30 * time.Minute\n420\t\n421\t\tif len(history) < 2 {\n422\t\t\treturn nil\n423\t\t}\n424\t\n425\t\t// Check if we've had enough time (handles both ascending and descending order)\n426\t\tstartTime := history[0].Timestamp\n427\t\tendTime := history[len(history)-1].Timestamp\n428\t\tduration := endTime.Sub(startTime)\n429\t\tif duration < 0 {\n430\t\t\tduration = -duration\n431\t\t}\n432\t\n433\t\tif duration < minDuration {\n434\t\t\treturn nil\n435\t\t}\n436\t\n437\t\t// Count samples during quiet periods with high phase instability\n438\t\tunstableQuietCount := 0\n439\t\ttotalQuietCount := 0\n440\t\n441\t\tfor _, h := range history {\n442\t\t\tif h.IsQuietPeriod {\n443\t\t\t\ttotalQuietCount++\n444\t\t\t\tif h.PhaseStability > phaseThreshold {\n445\t\t\t\t\tunstableQuietCount++\n446\t\t\t\t}\n447\t\t\t}\n448\t\t}\n449\t\n450\t\t// Need enough quiet period samples\n451\t\tif totalQuietCount < 10 {\n452\t\t\treturn nil\n453\t\t}\n454\t\n455\t\t// Require > 70% of quiet period samples to have instability\n456\t\tunstableRatio := float64(unstableQuietCount) / float64(totalQuietCount)\n457\t\tif unstableRatio < 0.7 {\n458\t\t\treturn nil\n459\t\t}\n460\t\n461\t\tnodeAMAC := extractNodeAMAC(linkID)\n462\t\n463\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n464\t\t\tLinkID:           linkID,\n465\t\t\tRuleID:           \"metal_interference\",\n466\t\t\tSeverity:         SeverityACTIONABLE,\n467\t\t\tTitle:            formatMetalTitle(nodeAMAC),\n468\t\t\tDetail:           formatMetalDetail(linkID),\n469\t\t\tAdvice:           formatMetalAdvice(nodeAMAC),\n470\t\t\tRepositioningTarget: nil,\n471\t\t\tRepositioningNodeMAC: nodeAMAC,\n472\t\t\tConfidenceScore:  0.80,\n473\t\t\tTimestamp:        time.Now(),\n474\t\t}\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\t// Rule 4: Fresnel Zone Blockage (Half-Room Dead Zone)\n478\t// Trigger: Consistent miss rate (>30% of test walks that should be detected are missed)\n479\t// in a specific area of the room.\n480\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) checkFresnelBlockage(linkID string, history []LinkHealthSnapshot) *Diagnosis {\n481\t\tde.mu.RLock()\n482\t\tgetFeedbackEvents := de.getFeedbackEvents\n483\t\tcomputeRepositioning := de.computeRepositioning\n484\t\tgetNodePosition := de.getNodePosition\n485\t\tde.mu.RUnlock()\n486\t\n487\t\tif getFeedbackEvents == nil {\n488\t\t\t// No feedback data - try heuristic approach\n489\t\t\treturn de.checkFresnelBlockageHeuristic(linkID, history)\n490\t\t}\n491\t\n492\t\t// Get recent false negative feedback events\n493\t\tevents := getFeedbackEvents(linkID, 7*24*time.Hour) // Last 7 days\n494\t\tif len(events) < 5 {\n495\t\t\t// Not enough feedback data\n496\t\t\treturn de.checkFresnelBlockageHeuristic(linkID, history)\n497\t\t}\n498\t\n499\t\t// Cluster false negatives by position\n500\t\tfalseNegatives := make([]FeedbackEvent, 0)\n501\t\tfor _, e := range events {\n502\t\t\tif e.EventType == \"false_negative\" {\n503\t\t\t\tfalseNegatives = append(falseNegatives, e)\n504\t\t\t}\n505\t\t}\n506\t\n507\t\tif len(falseNegatives) < 3 {\n508\t\t\treturn nil\n509\t\t}\n510\t\n511\t\t// Find the center of the blocked zone\n512\t\tblockedZone := findClusterCenter(falseNegatives)\n513\t\n514\t\t// Compute optimal repositioning target\n515\t\tvar target *Vec3\n516\t\tvar improvement float64\n517\t\tvar targetNodeMAC string\n518\t\n519\t\tif computeRepositioning != nil {\n520\t\t\tpos, imp, err := computeRepositioning(linkID, blockedZone)\n521\t\t\tif err == nil && imp > 0.1 {\n522\t\t\t\ttarget = &pos\n523\t\t\t\timprovement = imp\n524\t\t\t\ttargetNodeMAC = extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n525\t\t\t}\n526\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\t// Verify node position access for description\n529\t\tvar zoneDesc string\n530\t\tif getNodePosition != nil {\n531\t\t\tzoneDesc = formatZoneDescription(blockedZone)\n532\t\t} else {\n533\t\t\tzoneDesc = \"one side of the room\"\n534\t\t}\n535\t\n536\t\tnodeBMAC := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n537\t\tdetail := formatFresnelDetail(linkID, zoneDesc)\n538\t\tadvice := formatFresnelAdvice(nodeBMAC, &blockedZone, target, improvement)\n539\t\n540\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n541\t\t\tLinkID:              linkID,\n542\t\t\tRuleID:              \"fresnel_blockage\",\n543\t\t\tSeverity:            SeverityACTIONABLE,\n544\t\t\tTitle:               \"Coverage gap detected - possible obstruction\",\n545\t\t\tDetail:              detail,\n546\t\t\tAdvice:              advice,\n547\t\t\tRepositioningTarget: target,\n548\t\t\tRepositioningNodeMAC: targetNodeMAC,\n549\t\t\tConfidenceScore:     0.75,\n550\t\t\tTimestamp:           time.Now(),\n551\t\t}\n552\t}\n553\t\n554\t// checkFresnelBlockageHeuristic uses blob confidence data when no feedback is available\n555\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) checkFresnelBlockageHeuristic(linkID string, history []LinkHealthSnapshot) *Diagnosis {\n556\t\t// Heuristic: look for consistent low composite scores during activity periods\n557\t\t// This would require additional data from the fusion/localization engine\n558\t\t// For now, we'll check if there's consistently degraded health during non-quiet periods\n559\t\n560\t\tvar activityScores []float64\n561\t\tvar quietScores []float64\n562\t\n563\t\tfor _, h := range history {\n564\t\t\tif h.IsQuietPeriod {\n565\t\t\t\tquietScores = append(quietScores, h.CompositeScore)\n566\t\t\t} else {\n567\t\t\t\tactivityScores = append(activityScores, h.CompositeScore)\n568\t\t\t}\n569\t\t}\n570\t\n571\t\t// Need enough activity period data\n572\t\tif len(activityScores) < 10 {\n573\t\t\treturn nil\n574\t\t}\n575\t\n576\t\t// Calculate average scores\n577\t\tavgActivity := average(activityScores)\n578\t\tavgQuiet := average(quietScores)\n579\t\n580\t\t// If activity scores are significantly worse than quiet scores,\n581\t\t// there may be detection issues during movement\n582\t\tif avgActivity < 0.5 && avgActivity < avgQuiet-0.2 {\n583\t\t\tnodeBMAC := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n584\t\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n585\t\t\t\tLinkID:           linkID,\n586\t\t\t\tRuleID:           \"fresnel_blockage_heuristic\",\n587\t\t\t\tSeverity:         SeverityWARNING,\n588\t\t\t\tTitle:            \"Possible coverage gap detected\",\n589\t\t\t\tDetail:           \"Detection quality degrades during movement periods. This may indicate an obstruction in the sensing zone.\",\n590\t\t\t\tAdvice:           \"Submit feedback using the app when detection misses occur. This will help identify the exact location of the coverage gap.\",\n591\t\t\t\tRepositioningTarget: nil,\n592\t\t\t\tRepositioningNodeMAC: nodeBMAC,\n593\t\t\t\tConfidenceScore:  0.60, // Lower confidence for heuristic\n594\t\t\t\tTimestamp:        time.Now(),\n595\t\t\t}\n596\t\t}\n597\t\n598\t\treturn nil\n599\t}\n600\t\n601\t// Rule 5: Periodic Interference Spikes\n602\t// Trigger: Periodic spikes in deltaRMS variance (3-10 events per hour, each lasting 1-3 minutes)\n603\t// not correlated with occupancy data.\n604\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) checkPeriodicInterference(linkID string, history []LinkHealthSnapshot) *Diagnosis {\n605\t\t// Look for periodic variance spikes\n606\t\tminEvents := 3\n607\t\tmaxEvents := 10\n608\t\n609\t\t// Find variance spikes\n610\t\tspikes := findVarianceSpikes(history, 2.0) // 2x normal variance\n611\t\n612\t\tif len(spikes) < minEvents || len(spikes) > maxEvents*3 { // Allow some tolerance\n613\t\t\treturn nil\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\t// Check for periodicity (events occur every 6-20 minutes for 3-10 per hour)\n617\t\tif !isPeriodic(spikes, 1*time.Minute, 20*time.Minute) {\n618\t\t\treturn nil\n619\t\t}\n620\t\n621\t\t// Calculate events per hour (handles both ascending and descending order)\n622\t\tstartTime := history[0].Timestamp\n623\t\tendTime := history[len(history)-1].Timestamp\n624\t\thistoryDuration := endTime.Sub(startTime)\n625\t\tif historyDuration < 0 {\n626\t\t\thistoryDuration = -historyDuration\n627\t\t}\n628\t\n629\t\tif historyDuration < time.Hour {\n630\t\t\treturn nil // Need at least an hour of data\n631\t\t}\n632\t\n633\t\teventsPerHour := float64(len(spikes)) / historyDuration.Hours()\n634\t\tif eventsPerHour < float64(minEvents) || eventsPerHour > float64(maxEvents) {\n635\t\t\treturn nil\n636\t\t}\n637\t\n638\t\tnodeAMAC := extractNodeAMAC(linkID)\n639\t\tnodeBMAC := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n640\t\n641\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n642\t\t\tLinkID:           linkID,\n643\t\t\tRuleID:           \"periodic_interference\",\n644\t\t\tSeverity:         SeverityWARNING,\n645\t\t\tTitle:            \"Periodic interference detected\",\n646\t\t\tDetail:           formatInterferenceDetail(nodeAMAC, nodeBMAC, int(eventsPerHour)),\n647\t\t\tAdvice:           formatInterferenceAdvice(nodeAMAC, nodeBMAC),\n648\t\t\tRepositioningTarget: nil,\n649\t\t\tConfidenceScore:  0.70,\n650\t\t\tTimestamp:        time.Now(),\n651\t\t}\n652\t}\n653\t\n654\t// storeDiagnoses stores recent diagnoses for a link\n655\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) storeDiagnoses(linkID string, diagnoses []Diagnosis) {\n656\t\tde.mu.Lock()\n657\t\tdefer de.mu.Unlock()\n658\t\n659\t\tde.recentDiagnoses[linkID] = append(diagnoses, de.recentDiagnoses[linkID]...)\n660\t\tif len(de.recentDiagnoses[linkID]) > de.maxDiagnoses {\n661\t\t\tde.recentDiagnoses[linkID] = de.recentDiagnoses[linkID][:de.maxDiagnoses]\n662\t\t}\n663\t}\n664\t\n665\t// GetDiagnoses returns recent diagnoses for a link\n666\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) GetDiagnoses(linkID string) []Diagnosis {\n667\t\tde.mu.RLock()\n668\t\tdefer de.mu.RUnlock()\n669\t\n670\t\tdiagnoses := de.recentDiagnoses[linkID]\n671\t\tresult := make([]Diagnosis, len(diagnoses))\n672\t\tcopy(result, diagnoses)\n673\t\treturn result\n674\t}\n675\t\n676\t// GetAllDiagnoses returns all recent diagnoses\n677\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) GetAllDiagnoses() map[string][]Diagnosis {\n678\t\tde.mu.RLock()\n679\t\tdefer de.mu.RUnlock()\n680\t\n681\t\tresult := make(map[string][]Diagnosis)\n682\t\tfor linkID, diagnoses := range de.recentDiagnoses {\n683\t\t\tresult[linkID] = make([]Diagnosis, len(diagnoses))\n684\t\t\tcopy(result[linkID], diagnoses)\n685\t\t}\n686\t\treturn result\n687\t}\n688\t\n689\t// RunDiagnosticPass triggers an immediate diagnostic pass (for testing)\n690\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) RunDiagnosticPass() {\n691\t\tde.runDiagnosticPass()\n692\t}\n693\t\n694\t// Helper functions\n695\t\n696\tfunc calculateAverageDrift(history []LinkHealthSnapshot) float64 {\n697\t\tif len(history) == 0 {\n698\t\t\treturn 0\n699\t\t}\n700\t\tvar sum float64\n701\t\tfor _, h := range history {\n702\t\t\tsum += h.DriftRate\n703\t\t}\n704\t\treturn sum / float64(len(history))\n705\t}\n706\t\n707\tfunc average(values []float64) float64 {\n708\t\tif len(values) == 0 {\n709\t\t\treturn 0\n710\t\t}\n711\t\tvar sum float64\n712\t\tfor _, v := range values {\n713\t\t\tsum += v\n714\t\t}\n715\t\treturn sum / float64(len(values))\n716\t}\n717\t\n718\tfunc extractNodeAMAC(linkID string) string {\n719\t\tif len(linkID) < 17 {\n720\t\t\treturn \"\"\n721\t\t}\n722\t\treturn linkID[:17]\n723\t}\n724\t\n725\tfunc extractNodeBMAC(linkID string) string {\n726\t\tif len(linkID) < 35 {\n727\t\t\treturn \"\"\n728\t\t}\n729\t\treturn linkID[18:]\n730\t}\n731\t\n732\tfunc findClusterCenter(events []FeedbackEvent) Vec3 {\n733\t\tif len(events) == 0 {\n734\t\t\treturn Vec3{}\n735\t\t}\n736\t\n737\t\tvar sumX, sumY, sumZ float64\n738\t\tfor _, e := range events {\n739\t\t\tsumX += e.Position.X\n740\t\t\tsumY += e.Position.Y\n741\t\t\tsumZ += e.Position.Z\n742\t\t}\n743\t\tn := float64(len(events))\n744\t\treturn Vec3{X: sumX / n, Y: sumY / n, Z: sumZ / n}\n745\t}\n746\t\n747\tfunc findVarianceSpikes(history []LinkHealthSnapshot, threshold float64) []time.Time {\n748\t\tif len(history) == 0 {\n749\t\t\treturn nil\n750\t\t}\n751\t\n752\t\t// Calculate baseline variance\n753\t\tvar sum float64\n754\t\tfor _, h := range history {\n755\t\t\tsum += h.DeltaRMSVariance\n756\t\t}\n757\t\tbaseline := sum / float64(len(history))\n758\t\n759\t\tif baseline == 0 {\n760\t\t\tbaseline = 1 // Avoid division by zero\n761\t\t}\n762\t\n763\t\t// First, identify all high-variance samples with their timestamps\n764\t\ttype spikeSample struct {\n765\t\t\tts       time.Time\n766\t\t\tvariance float64\n767\t\t}\n768\t\tvar allSpikes []spikeSample\n769\t\tfor _, h := range history {\n770\t\t\tif h.DeltaRMSVariance > baseline*threshold {\n771\t\t\t\tallSpikes = append(allSpikes, spikeSample{ts: h.Timestamp, variance: h.DeltaRMSVariance})\n772\t\t\t}\n773\t\t}\n774\t\n775\t\tif len(allSpikes) == 0 {\n776\t\t\treturn nil\n777\t\t}\n778\t\n779\t\t// Sort spikes by timestamp\n780\t\tsort.Slice(allSpikes, func(i, j int) bool {\n781\t\t\treturn allSpikes[i].ts.Before(allSpikes[j].ts)\n782\t\t})\n783\t\n784\t\t// Cluster consecutive spikes (within 2 minutes) into events\n785\t\t// Return the start time of each event\n786\t\tvar events []time.Time\n787\t\teventStart := allSpikes[0].ts\n788\t\tlastSpikeTime := allSpikes[0].ts\n789\t\n790\t\tfor i := 1; i < len(allSpikes); i++ {\n791\t\t\t// If this spike is more than 2 minutes after the last, it's a new event\n792\t\t\tif allSpikes[i].ts.Sub(lastSpikeTime) > 2*time.Minute {\n793\t\t\t\tevents = append(events, eventStart)\n794\t\t\t\teventStart = allSpikes[i].ts\n795\t\t\t}\n796\t\t\tlastSpikeTime = allSpikes[i].ts\n797\t\t}\n798\t\t// Add the last event\n799\t\tevents = append(events, eventStart)\n800\t\n801\t\treturn events\n802\t}\n803\t\n804\tfunc isPeriodic(spikes []time.Time, minInterval, maxInterval time.Duration) bool {\n805\t\tif len(spikes) < 3 {\n806\t\t\treturn false\n807\t\t}\n808\t\n809\t\t// Sort spikes by timestamp to handle any order\n810\t\tsortedSpikes := make([]time.Time, len(spikes))\n811\t\tcopy(sortedSpikes, spikes)\n812\t\tsort.Slice(sortedSpikes, func(i, j int) bool {\n813\t\t\treturn sortedSpikes[i].Before(sortedSpikes[j])\n814\t\t})\n815\t\n816\t\t// Calculate intervals between spikes\n817\t\tintervals := make([]time.Duration, len(sortedSpikes)-1)\n818\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(sortedSpikes)-1; i++ {\n819\t\t\tintervals[i] = sortedSpikes[i+1].Sub(sortedSpikes[i])\n820\t\t}\n821\t\n822\t\t// Check if intervals are relatively consistent (within 50% of each other)\n823\t\tif len(intervals) == 0 {\n824\t\t\treturn false\n825\t\t}\n826\t\n827\t\tavgInterval := averageDurations(intervals)\n828\t\tif avgInterval < minInterval || avgInterval > maxInterval*3 {\n829\t\t\treturn false\n830\t\t}\n831\t\n832\t\t// Count intervals within tolerance of average\n833\t\ttolerance := 0.5\n834\t\twithinTolerance := 0\n835\t\tfor _, interval := range intervals {\n836\t\t\tratio := float64(interval) / float64(avgInterval)\n837\t\t\tif ratio > (1-tolerance) && ratio < (1+tolerance) {\n838\t\t\t\twithinTolerance++\n839\t\t\t}\n840\t\t}\n841\t\n842\t\t// At least 60% of intervals should be within tolerance\n843\t\treturn float64(withinTolerance)/float64(len(intervals)) >= 0.6\n844\t}\n845\t\n846\tfunc averageDurations(durations []time.Duration) time.Duration {\n847\t\tif len(durations) == 0 {\n848\t\t\treturn 0\n849\t\t}\n850\t\tvar sum time.Duration\n851\t\tfor _, d := range durations {\n852\t\t\tsum += d\n853\t\t}\n854\t\treturn sum / time.Duration(len(durations))\n855\t}\n856\t\n857\t// Formatting helpers\n858\t\n859\tfunc formatWiFiDetail(nodeMAC string, avgPacketRate float64) string {\n860\t\tpct := int(avgPacketRate / 20.0 * 100)\n861\t\tif nodeMAC != \"\" {\n862\t\t\treturn formatNodeLabel(nodeMAC) + \" is only delivering \" + formatInt(pct) + \"% of the expected 20 packets per second. The most common causes are distance from the WiFi router or congestion from nearby networks.\"\n863\t\t}\n864\t\treturn \"This link is only delivering \" + formatInt(pct) + \"% of the expected packets. The most common causes are distance from the WiFi router or congestion from nearby networks.\"\n865\t}\n866\t\n867\tfunc formatWiFiAdvice(nodeMAC string) string {\n868\t\tlabel := \"the node\"\n869\t\tif nodeMAC != \"\" {\n870\t\t\tlabel = formatNodeLabel(nodeMAC)\n871\t\t}\n872\t\treturn \"1. Move \" + label + \" within 10 metres of your WiFi router. \" +\n873\t\t\t\"2. If already close, check if the 2.4GHz channel is congested (3+ networks on overlapping channels). \" +\n874\t\t\t\"3. ESP32-S3 supports both 2.4GHz and 5GHz - if your router supports 5GHz, update the node's WiFi config to use the 5GHz SSID.\"\n875\t}\n876\t\n877\tfunc formatMetalTitle(nodeMAC string) string {\n878\t\tif nodeMAC != \"\" {\n879\t\t\treturn \"Metal interference near \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeMAC)\n880\t\t}\n881\t\treturn \"Metal interference detected\"\n882\t}\n883\t\n884\tfunc formatMetalDetail(linkID string) string {\n885\t\tnodeA := extractNodeAMAC(linkID)\n886\t\tnodeB := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n887\t\tif nodeA != \"\" && nodeB != \"\" {\n888\t\t\treturn \"The sensing link \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeA) + \" to \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeB) + \" has unstable phase measurements even when no one is moving. This is typically caused by metal objects in the near field of the node's antenna (within 10cm): metal shelves, radiators, TV backs, or large appliances.\"\n889\t\t}\n890\t\treturn \"This sensing link has unstable phase measurements even when no one is moving. This is typically caused by metal objects in the near field of the node's antenna (within 10cm).\"\n891\t}\n892\t\n893\tfunc formatMetalAdvice(nodeMAC string) string {\n894\t\tlabel := \"the node\"\n895\t\tif nodeMAC != \"\" {\n896\t\t\tlabel = formatNodeLabel(nodeMAC)\n897\t\t}\n898\t\treturn \"Check for metal objects within 10cm of \" + label + \". \" +\n899\t\t\t\"If \" + label + \" is on a metal surface or shelf, mount it on a non-metal bracket or wall. \" +\n900\t\t\t\"Try repositioning it 20-30cm away from metal surfaces.\"\n901\t}\n902\t\n903\tfunc formatFresnelDetail(linkID, zoneDesc string) string {\n904\t\tnodeA := extractNodeAMAC(linkID)\n905\t\tnodeB := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n906\t\tif nodeA != \"\" && nodeB != \"\" {\n907\t\t\treturn \"The area near \" + zoneDesc + \" shows lower detection coverage. An obstacle may be blocking the path between \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeA) + \" and \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeB) + \", interrupting their sensing zone.\"\n908\t\t}\n909\t\treturn \"The area near \" + zoneDesc + \" shows lower detection coverage. An obstacle may be blocking the sensing zone.\"\n910\t}\n911\t\n912\tfunc formatFresnelAdvice(nodeMAC string, blockedZone, target *Vec3, improvement float64) string {\n913\t\tif target == nil || nodeMAC == \"\" {\n914\t\t\treturn \"Check for obstructions between the nodes. Large furniture, appliances, or metal objects can block the sensing zone.\"\n915\t\t}\n916\t\n917\t\t// Calculate direction from current position\n918\t\tdirection := calculateDirection(blockedZone, target)\n919\t\tdistance := calculateDistance(blockedZone, target)\n920\t\n921\t\tadvice := \"Move \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeMAC) + \" \" + direction + \" by approximately \" + formatDistance(distance) + \" to restore coverage.\"\n922\t\n923\t\tif improvement > 0 {\n924\t\t\tadvice += \" The target position is marked in green in the 3D view.\"\n925\t\t}\n926\t\n927\t\treturn advice\n928\t}\n929\t\n930\tfunc formatInterferenceDetail(nodeA, nodeB string, eventsPerHour int) string {\n931\t\tif nodeA != \"\" && nodeB != \"\" {\n932\t\t\treturn formatNodeLabel(nodeA) + \" to \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeB) + \" is experiencing regular interference bursts \" + formatInt(eventsPerHour) + \" times per hour. This pattern is consistent with a microwave oven, a cordless phone, or a pulsed 2.4GHz source.\"\n933\t\t}\n934\t\treturn \"This link is experiencing regular interference bursts \" + formatInt(eventsPerHour) + \" times per hour. This pattern is consistent with a microwave oven, a cordless phone, or a pulsed 2.4GHz source.\"\n935\t}\n936\t\n937\tfunc formatInterferenceAdvice(nodeA, nodeB string) string {\n938\t\tnodes := \"the nodes\"\n939\t\tif nodeA != \"\" && nodeB != \"\" {\n940\t\t\tnodes = formatNodeLabel(nodeA) + \" or \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeB)\n941\t\t}\n942\t\treturn \"Consider the following: \" +\n943\t\t\t\"1. Is \" + nodes + \" near a kitchen? Microwave ovens cause strong 2.4GHz interference. \" +\n944\t\t\t\"2. A cordless DECT phone or baby monitor near one of the nodes may be the source. \" +\n945\t\t\t\"3. Try moving the affected node at least 2 metres from any 2.4GHz appliances.\"\n946\t}\n947\t\n948\tfunc formatZoneDescription(pos Vec3) string {\n949\t\t// Simple zone description based on position\n950\t\t// This could be enhanced with room-relative positioning\n951\t\treturn formatFloat(pos.X, 1) + \"m, \" + formatFloat(pos.Z, 1) + \"m from origin\"\n952\t}\n953\t\n954\tfunc formatNodeLabel(mac string) string {\n955\t\tif len(mac) >= 8 {\n956\t\t\treturn \"Node \" + mac[:8]\n957\t\t}\n958\t\treturn \"Node\"\n959\t}\n960\t\n961\tfunc calculateDirection(from, to *Vec3) string {\n962\t\tif from == nil || to == nil {\n963\t\t\treturn \"\"\n964\t\t}\n965\t\n966\t\tdx := to.X - from.X\n967\t\tdz := to.Z - from.Z\n968\t\n969\t\tvar direction string\n970\t\tif math.Abs(dz) > math.Abs(dx) {\n971\t\t\tif dz > 0 {\n972\t\t\t\tdirection = \"forward\"\n973\t\t\t} else {\n974\t\t\t\tdirection = \"backward\"\n975\t\t\t}\n976\t\t} else {\n977\t\t\tif dx > 0 {\n978\t\t\t\tdirection = \"right\"\n979\t\t\t} else {\n980\t\t\t\tdirection = \"left\"\n981\t\t\t}\n982\t\t}\n983\t\n984\t\treturn direction\n985\t}\n986\t\n987\tfunc calculateDistance(from, to *Vec3) float64 {\n988\t\tif from == nil || to == nil {\n989\t\t\treturn 0\n990\t\t}\n991\t\tdx := to.X - from.X\n992\t\tdz := to.Z - from.Z\n993\t\treturn math.Sqrt(dx*dx + dz*dz)\n994\t}\n995\t\n996\tfunc formatDistance(m float64) string {\n997\t\tif m < 0.5 {\n998\t\t\treturn formatFloat(m*100, 0) + \"cm\"\n999\t\t}\n1000\t\treturn formatFloat(m, 1) + \" metres\"\n1001\t}\n1002\t\n1003\tfunc formatInt(n int) string {\n1004\t\tif n < 0 {\n1005\t\t\treturn \"0\"\n1006\t\t}\n1007\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", n)\n1008\t}\n1009\t\n1010\tfunc formatFloat(f float64, decimals int) string {\n1011\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%.[2]*[1]f\", f, decimals)\n1012\t}\n1013\t\n1014\t// Tooltip represents a contextual help tooltip for a feature.\n1015\ttype Tooltip struct {\n1016\t\tTitle       string\n1017\t\tDescription string\n1018\t\tDirection   string // \"top\", \"bottom\", \"left\", \"right\"\n1019\t}\n1020\t\n1021\t// GetDiagnosticFor returns diagnostic information for a specific link at a given timestamp.\n1022\t// This is used by the post-feedback explanation system to provide root cause analysis\n1023\t// for false positive detections.\n1024\tfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) GetDiagnosticFor(linkID string, timestamp time.Time) *Diagnosis {\n1025\t\tde.mu.RLock()\n1026\t\tgetHealthHistory := de.getHealthHistory\n1027\t\twindow := de.config.HistoryWindow\n1028\t\tde.mu.RUnlock()\n1029\t\n1030\t\tif getHealthHistory == nil {\n1031\t\t\treturn nil\n1032\t\t}\n1033\t\n1034\t\t// Get health history around the specified timestamp\n1035\t\thistory := getHealthHistory(linkID, window)\n1036\t\tif len(history) < de.config.MinSamples {\n1037\t\t\t// Not enough data - return a generic diagnosis\n1038\t\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n1039\t\t\t\tLinkID:           linkID,\n1040\t\t\t\tRuleID:           \"insufficient_data\",\n1041\t\t\t\tSeverity:         SeverityINFO,\n1042\t\t\t\tTitle:            \"Not enough data for diagnosis\",\n1043\t\t\t\tDetail:           \"This link hasn't been active long enough to analyze.\",\n1044\t\t\t\tAdvice:           \"Continue normal operation. Diagnostics will be available after more data is collected.\",\n1045\t\t\t\tConfidenceScore:  0.0,\n1046\t\t\t\tTimestamp:        time.Now(),\n1047\t\t\t}\n1048\t\t}\n1049\t\n1050\t\t// Find the snapshot closest to the requested timestamp\n1051\t\tvar closestSnapshot *LinkHealthSnapshot\n1052\t\tminDiff := time.Duration(1<<63 - 1) // Max duration\n1053\t\n1054\t\tfor i := range history {\n1055\t\t\tdiff := history[i].Timestamp.Sub(timestamp)\n1056\t\t\tif diff < 0 {\n1057\t\t\t\tdiff = -diff\n1058\t\t\t}\n1059\t\t\tif diff < minDiff {\n1060\t\t\t\tminDiff = diff\n1061\t\t\t\tclosestSnapshot = &history[i]\n1062\t\t\t}\n1063\t\t}\n1064\t\n1065\t\tif closestSnapshot == nil {\n1066\t\t\treturn nil\n1067\t\t}\n1068\t\n1069\t\t// Check which diagnostic rules would have fired at that time\n1070\t\t// We'll run a focused diagnostic check around the timestamp\n1071\t\n1072\t\t// Rule: Check for environmental change indicators\n1073\t\tif closestSnapshot.DriftRate > 0.05 {\n1074\t\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n1075\t\t\t\tLinkID:   linkID,\n1076\t\t\t\tRuleID:   \"environmental_change\",\n1077\t\t\t\tSeverity: SeverityINFO,\n1078\t\t\t\tTitle:    \"Possible environmental change\",\n1079\t\t\t\tDetail:   \"The baseline for this link has drifted significantly. This could be caused by temperature changes, furniture movement, or new obstructions.\",\n1080\t\t\t\tAdvice:   \"The system is adapting automatically. No action needed unless this persists.\",\n1081\t\t\t\tConfidenceScore: 0.70,\n1082\t\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n1083\t\t\t}\n1084\t\t}\n1085\t\n1086\t\t// Rule: Check for WiFi congestion indicators\n1087\t\tif closestSnapshot.PacketRate < 16.0 { // Less than 80% of expected 20 Hz\n1088\t\t\tnodeBMAC := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n1089\t\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n1090\t\t\t\tLinkID:   linkID,\n1091\t\t\t\tRuleID:   \"wifi_congestion\",\n1092\t\t\t\tSeverity: SeverityACTIONABLE,\n1093\t\t\t\tTitle:    \"Possible WiFi congestion\",\n1094\t\t\t\tDetail:   formatWiFiDetail(nodeBMAC, closestSnapshot.PacketRate),\n1095\t\t\t\tAdvice:   formatWiFiAdvice(nodeBMAC),\n1096\t\t\t\tRepositioningNodeMAC: nodeBMAC,\n1097\t\t\t\tConfidenceScore: 0.75,\n1098\t\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n1099\t\t\t}\n1100\t\t}\n1101\t\n1102\t\t// Rule: Check for phase instability (metal interference)\n1103\t\tif closestSnapshot.PhaseStability > 0.6 {\n1104\t\t\tnodeAMAC := extractNodeAMAC(linkID)\n1105\t\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n1106\t\t\t\tLinkID:   linkID,\n1107\t\t\t\tRuleID:   \"metal_interference\",\n1108\t\t\t\tSeverity: SeverityACTIONABLE,\n1109\t\t\t\tTitle:    formatMetalTitle(nodeAMAC),\n1110\t\t\t\tDetail:   formatMetalDetail(linkID),\n1111\t\t\t\tAdvice:   formatMetalAdvice(nodeAMAC),\n1112\t\t\t\tRepositioningNodeMAC: nodeAMAC,\n1113\t\t\t\tConfidenceScore: 0.80,\n1114\t\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n1115\t\t\t}\n1116\t\t}\n1117\t\n1118\t\t// Rule: Check for periodic interference patterns\n1119\t\tif closestSnapshot.DeltaRMSVariance > 2.0 {\n1120\t\t\tnodeAMAC := extractNodeAMAC(linkID)\n1121\t\t\tnodeBMAC := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n1122\t\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n1123\t\t\t\tLinkID:   linkID,\n1124\t\t\t\tRuleID:   \"periodic_interference\",\n1125\t\t\t\tSeverity: SeverityWARNING,\n1126\t\t\t\tTitle:    \"Possible periodic interference\",\n1127\t\t\t\tDetail:   formatInterferenceDetail(nodeAMAC, nodeBMAC, 3),\n1128\t\t\t\tAdvice:   formatInterferenceAdvice(nodeAMAC, nodeBMAC),\n1129\t\t\t\tConfidenceScore: 0.70,\n1130\t\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n1131\t\t\t}\n1132\t\t}\n1133\t\n1134\t\t// Default: no specific issue found\n1135\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n1136\t\t\tLinkID:   linkID,\n1137\t\t\tRuleID:   \"no_issue_detected\",\n1138\t\t\tSeverity: SeverityINFO,\n1139\t\t\tTitle:    \"No specific issue detected\",\n1140\t\t\tDetail:   \"The link health metrics appear normal. The false positive may be due to transient RF interference or a brief environmental change.\",\n1141\t\t\tAdvice:   \"This is a rare occurrence. If it happens frequently, consider submitting feedback so the system can learn.\",\n1142\t\t\tConfidenceScore: 0.50,\n1143\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n1144\t\t}\n1145\t}\n1146\t\n1147\t// GetDiagnosisExplanation returns a plain-language explanation for a diagnosis.\n1148\tfunc (d *Diagnosis) GetDiagnosisExplanation() string {\n1149\t\tif d == nil {\n1150\t\t\treturn \"No diagnostic information available.\"\n1151\t\t}\n1152\t\n1153\t\texplanation := d.Detail\n1154\t\n1155\t\t// Add context-specific advice\n1156\t\tif d.RuleID == \"environmental_change\" {\n1157\t\t\texplanation += \" The system adapts to these changes automatically over time.\"\n1158\t\t} else if d.RuleID == \"wifi_congestion\" {\n1159\t\t\texplanation += \" Moving the node closer to your WiFi router may help.\"\n1160\t\t} else if d.RuleID == \"metal_interference\" {\n1161\t\t\texplanation += \" Check for metal objects within 10cm of the node antenna.\"\n1162\t\t} else if d.RuleID == \"periodic_interference\" {\n1163\t\t\texplanation += \" This pattern often indicates a microwave oven or other 2.4GHz device.\"\n1164\t\t}\n1165\t\n1166\t\treturn explanation\n1167\t}\n1168\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"46d760ba-5b40-408c-9802-144f5c4b71d7","uuid":"6b9f632e-2ede-4edd-a880-aba97760c3d4","timestamp":"2026-05-04T08:52:02.879Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/spaxel/mothership/internal/diagnostics/linkweather.go","content":"// Package diagnostics implements link weather diagnostics with root-cause analysis\n// and actionable repositioning advice.\npackage diagnostics\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"math\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// DiagnosisSeverity represents the severity level of a diagnosis\ntype DiagnosisSeverity string\n\nconst (\n\tSeverityINFO      DiagnosisSeverity = \"INFO\"      // Informational, no action needed\n\tSeverityWARNING   DiagnosisSeverity = \"WARNING\"   // Attention recommended\n\tSeverityACTIONABLE DiagnosisSeverity = \"ACTIONABLE\" // Specific action required\n)\n\n// Diagnosis represents a diagnostic finding for a link\ntype Diagnosis struct {\n\tLinkID              string\n\tRuleID              string            // Identifies which rule fired\n\tSeverity            DiagnosisSeverity // INFO, WARNING, ACTIONABLE\n\tTitle               string            // Human-readable headline\n\tDetail              string            // Explanation in plain language\n\tAdvice              string            // Specific actionable steps\n\tRepositioningTarget *Vec3             // 3D position to move node, or nil\n\tRepositioningNodeMAC string           // Which node to move\n\tConfidenceScore     float64           // How confident the engine is (0-1)\n\tTimestamp           time.Time\n}\n\n// Vec3 represents a 3D position\ntype Vec3 struct {\n\tX float64\n\tY float64\n\tZ float64\n}\n\n// LinkHealthSnapshot represents a health snapshot for diagnostic analysis\ntype LinkHealthSnapshot struct {\n\tTimestamp       time.Time\n\tSNR             float64\n\tPhaseStability  float64\n\tPacketRate      float64\n\tDriftRate       float64\n\tCompositeScore  float64\n\tDeltaRMSVariance float64 // For periodic interference detection\n\tIsQuietPeriod   bool     // True if no motion detected\n}\n\n// FeedbackEvent represents user-reported false negative/positive for Rule 4\ntype FeedbackEvent struct {\n\tLinkID    string\n\tEventType string    // \"false_negative\" or \"false_positive\"\n\tPosition  Vec3      // Where the event occurred\n\tTimestamp time.Time\n}\n\n// DiagnosticConfig holds configuration for the DiagnosticEngine\ntype DiagnosticConfig struct {\n\tDiagnosticInterval time.Duration // How often to run diagnostics (default 15m)\n\tHistoryWindow      time.Duration // How much history to analyze (default 1h)\n\tMinSamples         int           // Minimum samples needed for diagnosis\n}\n\n// DiagnosticEngine runs background diagnostics on link health\ntype DiagnosticEngine struct {\n\tmu sync.RWMutex\n\n\tconfig DiagnosticConfig\n\n\t// Health data access\n\tgetHealthHistory func(linkID string, window time.Duration) []LinkHealthSnapshot\n\tgetAllLinkIDs    func() []string\n\n\t// Feedback data access (for Rule 4)\n\tgetFeedbackEvents func(linkID string, window time.Duration) []FeedbackEvent\n\n\t// Repositioning computation (for Rule 4)\n\tcomputeRepositioning func(linkID string, blockedZone Vec3) (Vec3, float64, error)\n\n\t// GDOP calculator access\n\tgetGDOPImprovement func(nodeMAC string, targetPos Vec3) float64\n\n\t// Node position access\n\tgetNodePosition func(mac string) (Vec3, bool)\n\n\t// Occupancy state for quiet period detection\n\tgetOccupancyState func() int // Returns number of detected persons\n\n\t// Recent diagnoses per link (last N diagnoses)\n\trecentDiagnoses map[string][]Diagnosis\n\tmaxDiagnoses    int\n\n\t// Running state\n\trunning bool\n\tctx     context.Context\n\tcancel  context.CancelFunc\n}\n\n// NewDiagnosticEngine creates a new diagnostic engine\nfunc NewDiagnosticEngine(cfg DiagnosticConfig) *DiagnosticEngine {\n\tif cfg.DiagnosticInterval == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.DiagnosticInterval = 15 * time.Minute\n\t}\n\tif cfg.HistoryWindow == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.HistoryWindow = 1 * time.Hour\n\t}\n\tif cfg.MinSamples == 0 {\n\t\tcfg.MinSamples = 10\n\t}\n\n\treturn &DiagnosticEngine{\n\t\tconfig:          cfg,\n\t\trecentDiagnoses: make(map[string][]Diagnosis),\n\t\tmaxDiagnoses:    10,\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetHealthHistoryAccessor sets the function to get health history\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) SetHealthHistoryAccessor(fn func(linkID string, window time.Duration) []LinkHealthSnapshot) {\n\tde.mu.Lock()\n\tde.getHealthHistory = fn\n\tde.mu.Unlock()\n}\n\n// SetAllLinkIDsAccessor sets the function to get all link IDs\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) SetAllLinkIDsAccessor(fn func() []string) {\n\tde.mu.Lock()\n\tde.getAllLinkIDs = fn\n\tde.mu.Unlock()\n}\n\n// SetFeedbackAccessor sets the function to get feedback events\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) SetFeedbackAccessor(fn func(linkID string, window time.Duration) []FeedbackEvent) {\n\tde.mu.Lock()\n\tde.getFeedbackEvents = fn\n\tde.mu.Unlock()\n}\n\n// SetRepositioningComputer sets the function to compute repositioning targets\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) SetRepositioningComputer(fn func(linkID string, blockedZone Vec3) (Vec3, float64, error)) {\n\tde.mu.Lock()\n\tde.computeRepositioning = fn\n\tde.mu.Unlock()\n}\n\n// SetGDOPImprovementAccessor sets the function to get GDOP improvement estimates\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) SetGDOPImprovementAccessor(fn func(nodeMAC string, targetPos Vec3) float64) {\n\tde.mu.Lock()\n\tde.getGDOPImprovement = fn\n\tde.mu.Unlock()\n}\n\n// SetNodePositionAccessor sets the function to get node positions\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) SetNodePositionAccessor(fn func(mac string) (Vec3, bool)) {\n\tde.mu.Lock()\n\tde.getNodePosition = fn\n\tde.mu.Unlock()\n}\n\n// SetOccupancyAccessor sets the function to get occupancy state\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) SetOccupancyAccessor(fn func() int) {\n\tde.mu.Lock()\n\tde.getOccupancyState = fn\n\tde.mu.Unlock()\n}\n\n// Run starts the diagnostic engine background loop\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) Run(ctx context.Context) {\n\tde.mu.Lock()\n\tif de.running {\n\t\tde.mu.Unlock()\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tde.running = true\n\tde.ctx, de.cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)\n\tde.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(de.config.DiagnosticInterval)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] diagnostics: engine started (interval: %v)\", de.config.DiagnosticInterval)\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-de.ctx.Done():\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] diagnostics: engine stopped\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\tde.runDiagnosticPass()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// Stop stops the diagnostic engine\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) Stop() {\n\tde.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer de.mu.Unlock()\n\tif de.cancel != nil {\n\t\tde.cancel()\n\t}\n\tde.running = false\n}\n\n// runDiagnosticPass runs all diagnostic rules on all links\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) runDiagnosticPass() {\n\tde.mu.RLock()\n\tgetAllLinkIDs := de.getAllLinkIDs\n\tde.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif getAllLinkIDs == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tlinkIDs := getAllLinkIDs()\n\tfor _, linkID := range linkIDs {\n\t\tdiagnoses := de.diagnoseLink(linkID)\n\t\tif len(diagnoses) > 0 {\n\t\t\tde.storeDiagnoses(linkID, diagnoses)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// diagnoseLink runs all diagnostic rules on a single link\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) diagnoseLink(linkID string) []Diagnosis {\n\tde.mu.RLock()\n\tgetHealthHistory := de.getHealthHistory\n\twindow := de.config.HistoryWindow\n\tde.mu.RUnlock()\n\t_ = de.getFeedbackEvents\n\t_ = de.getOccupancyState\n\n\tif getHealthHistory == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\thistory := getHealthHistory(linkID, window)\n\tif len(history) < de.config.MinSamples {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar diagnoses []Diagnosis\n\n\t// Rule 1: Environmental Change\n\tif d := de.checkEnvironmentalChange(linkID, history); d != nil {\n\t\tdiagnoses = append(diagnoses, *d)\n\t}\n\n\t// Rule 2: WiFi Congestion or Distance\n\tif d := de.checkWiFiCongestion(linkID, history); d != nil {\n\t\tdiagnoses = append(diagnoses, *d)\n\t}\n\n\t// Rule 3: Near-Field Metal Interference\n\tif d := de.checkMetalInterference(linkID, history); d != nil {\n\t\tdiagnoses = append(diagnoses, *d)\n\t}\n\n\t// Rule 4: Fresnel Zone Blockage\n\tif d := de.checkFresnelBlockage(linkID, history); d != nil {\n\t\tdiagnoses = append(diagnoses, *d)\n\t}\n\n\t// Rule 5: Periodic Interference Spikes\n\tif d := de.checkPeriodicInterference(linkID, history); d != nil {\n\t\tdiagnoses = append(diagnoses, *d)\n\t}\n\n\treturn diagnoses\n}\n\n// Rule 1: Environmental Change\n// Trigger: High baseline drift (>5% per hour) correlated across multiple links simultaneously (>50% of active links).\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) checkEnvironmentalChange(linkID string, history []LinkHealthSnapshot) *Diagnosis {\n\tde.mu.RLock()\n\tgetAllLinkIDs := de.getAllLinkIDs\n\tgetHealthHistory := de.getHealthHistory\n\tde.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif getAllLinkIDs == nil || getHealthHistory == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate drift rate for this link\n\tavgDrift := calculateAverageDrift(history)\n\tif avgDrift < 0.05 { // < 5% per hour\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if drift is correlated across multiple links\n\tallLinks := getAllLinkIDs()\n\tcorrelatedCount := 0\n\ttotalActive := 0\n\n\tfor _, lid := range allLinks {\n\t\tlhistory := getHealthHistory(lid, de.config.HistoryWindow)\n\t\tif len(lhistory) < de.config.MinSamples {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\ttotalActive++\n\t\tlavgDrift := calculateAverageDrift(lhistory)\n\t\tif lavgDrift >= 0.05 {\n\t\t\tcorrelatedCount++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif totalActive == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tcorrelationRatio := float64(correlatedCount) / float64(totalActive)\n\tif correlationRatio < 0.5 {\n\t\treturn nil // Not correlated enough\n\t}\n\n\t// Confidence: 0.85 if drift is correlated across >50% of links\n\tconfidence := 0.85\n\n\treturn &Diagnosis{\n\t\tLinkID:           linkID,\n\t\tRuleID:           \"environmental_change\",\n\t\tSeverity:         SeverityINFO,\n\t\tTitle:            \"Environmental change detected\",\n\t\tDetail:           \"Multiple sensing links are showing simultaneous baseline shifts. This typically indicates a temperature change, or a large object was moved in the space. The system is adapting automatically.\",\n\t\tAdvice:           \"No action needed. The baseline will re-stabilise within 30 minutes.\",\n\t\tRepositioningTarget: nil,\n\t\tConfidenceScore:  confidence,\n\t\tTimestamp:        time.Now(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// Rule 2: WiFi Congestion or Distance\n// Trigger: Packet rate health < 0.8 for more than 10 minutes on a single link.\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) checkWiFiCongestion(linkID string, history []LinkHealthSnapshot) *Diagnosis {\n\t// Check if packet rate has been low for > 10 minutes\n\t// Expected rate is 20 Hz, so health < 0.8 means < 16 Hz\n\tpacketHealthThreshold := 0.8\n\tminDuration := 10 * time.Minute\n\n\tif len(history) < 2 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Find the time span of history (handles both ascending and descending order)\n\tstartTime := history[0].Timestamp\n\tendTime := history[len(history)-1].Timestamp\n\tduration := endTime.Sub(startTime)\n\tif duration < 0 {\n\t\tduration = -duration\n\t}\n\n\tif duration < minDuration {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if packet rate health has been consistently low\n\tlowCount := 0\n\ttotalCount := 0\n\tvar avgPacketRate float64\n\n\tfor _, h := range history {\n\t\t// Packet rate health: actual/expected (20 Hz)\n\t\trateHealth := h.PacketRate / 20.0\n\t\tavgPacketRate += h.PacketRate\n\t\ttotalCount++\n\t\tif rateHealth < packetHealthThreshold {\n\t\t\tlowCount++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif totalCount == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tavgPacketRate /= float64(totalCount)\n\tlowRatio := float64(lowCount) / float64(totalCount)\n\n\t// Require > 80% of samples to have low packet rate\n\tif lowRatio < 0.8 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract node B MAC from linkID\n\tnodeBMAC := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n\n\treturn &Diagnosis{\n\t\tLinkID:           linkID,\n\t\tRuleID:           \"wifi_congestion_distance\",\n\t\tSeverity:         SeverityACTIONABLE,\n\t\tTitle:            \"Node has low signal rate\",\n\t\tDetail:           formatWiFiDetail(nodeBMAC, avgPacketRate),\n\t\tAdvice: formatWiFiAdvice(nodeBMAC),\n\t\tRepositioningTarget: nil,\n\t\tRepositioningNodeMAC: nodeBMAC,\n\t\tConfidenceScore:  0.75,\n\t\tTimestamp:        time.Now(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// Rule 3: Near-Field Metal Interference\n// Trigger: Low phase stability (< 0.4) sustained for > 30 minutes during known-quiet periods.\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) checkMetalInterference(linkID string, history []LinkHealthSnapshot) *Diagnosis {\n\t// Phase stability: lower is more stable. We need to check if stability score is low.\n\t// In the codebase, PhaseStability is normalized variance where < 0.4 is stable\n\t// So \"low phase stability\" means the value is HIGH (> 0.6 or so)\n\t// Actually looking at ambient.go: stability score of 0.1 is very stable, 1.0 is unstable\n\t// The task says \"Low phase stability (< 0.4)\" but this is confusing.\n\t// Looking at weather.go: phaseStabilityWarn = 0.5, phaseStabilityCrit = 0.8\n\t// I'll interpret \"low phase stability\" as \"high phase variance\" i.e. PhaseStability > 0.6\n\n\tphaseThreshold := 0.6 // High variance = unstable\n\tminDuration := 30 * time.Minute\n\n\tif len(history) < 2 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if we've had enough time (handles both ascending and descending order)\n\tstartTime := history[0].Timestamp\n\tendTime := history[len(history)-1].Timestamp\n\tduration := endTime.Sub(startTime)\n\tif duration < 0 {\n\t\tduration = -duration\n\t}\n\n\tif duration < minDuration {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Count samples during quiet periods with high phase instability\n\tunstableQuietCount := 0\n\ttotalQuietCount := 0\n\n\tfor _, h := range history {\n\t\tif h.IsQuietPeriod {\n\t\t\ttotalQuietCount++\n\t\t\tif h.PhaseStability > phaseThreshold {\n\t\t\t\tunstableQuietCount++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Need enough quiet period samples\n\tif totalQuietCount < 10 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Require > 70% of quiet period samples to have instability\n\tunstableRatio := float64(unstableQuietCount) / float64(totalQuietCount)\n\tif unstableRatio < 0.7 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tnodeAMAC := extractNodeAMAC(linkID)\n\n\treturn &Diagnosis{\n\t\tLinkID:           linkID,\n\t\tRuleID:           \"metal_interference\",\n\t\tSeverity:         SeverityACTIONABLE,\n\t\tTitle:            formatMetalTitle(nodeAMAC),\n\t\tDetail:           formatMetalDetail(linkID),\n\t\tAdvice:           formatMetalAdvice(nodeAMAC),\n\t\tRepositioningTarget: nil,\n\t\tRepositioningNodeMAC: nodeAMAC,\n\t\tConfidenceScore:  0.80,\n\t\tTimestamp:        time.Now(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// Rule 4: Fresnel Zone Blockage (Half-Room Dead Zone)\n// Trigger: Consistent miss rate (>30% of test walks that should be detected are missed)\n// in a specific area of the room.\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) checkFresnelBlockage(linkID string, history []LinkHealthSnapshot) *Diagnosis {\n\tde.mu.RLock()\n\tgetFeedbackEvents := de.getFeedbackEvents\n\tcomputeRepositioning := de.computeRepositioning\n\tgetNodePosition := de.getNodePosition\n\tde.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif getFeedbackEvents == nil {\n\t\t// No feedback data - try heuristic approach\n\t\treturn de.checkFresnelBlockageHeuristic(linkID, history)\n\t}\n\n\t// Get recent false negative feedback events\n\tevents := getFeedbackEvents(linkID, 7*24*time.Hour) // Last 7 days\n\tif len(events) < 5 {\n\t\t// Not enough feedback data\n\t\treturn de.checkFresnelBlockageHeuristic(linkID, history)\n\t}\n\n\t// Cluster false negatives by position\n\tfalseNegatives := make([]FeedbackEvent, 0)\n\tfor _, e := range events {\n\t\tif e.EventType == \"false_negative\" {\n\t\t\tfalseNegatives = append(falseNegatives, e)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif len(falseNegatives) < 3 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Find the center of the blocked zone\n\tblockedZone := findClusterCenter(falseNegatives)\n\n\t// Compute optimal repositioning target\n\tvar target *Vec3\n\tvar improvement float64\n\tvar targetNodeMAC string\n\n\tif computeRepositioning != nil {\n\t\tpos, imp, err := computeRepositioning(linkID, blockedZone)\n\t\tif err == nil && imp > 0.1 {\n\t\t\ttarget = &pos\n\t\t\timprovement = imp\n\t\t\ttargetNodeMAC = extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify node position access for description\n\tvar zoneDesc string\n\tif getNodePosition != nil {\n\t\tzoneDesc = formatZoneDescription(blockedZone)\n\t} else {\n\t\tzoneDesc = \"one side of the room\"\n\t}\n\n\tnodeBMAC := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n\tdetail := formatFresnelDetail(linkID, zoneDesc)\n\tadvice := formatFresnelAdvice(nodeBMAC, &blockedZone, target, improvement)\n\n\treturn &Diagnosis{\n\t\tLinkID:              linkID,\n\t\tRuleID:              \"fresnel_blockage\",\n\t\tSeverity:            SeverityACTIONABLE,\n\t\tTitle:               \"Coverage gap detected - possible obstruction\",\n\t\tDetail:              detail,\n\t\tAdvice:              advice,\n\t\tRepositioningTarget: target,\n\t\tRepositioningNodeMAC: targetNodeMAC,\n\t\tConfidenceScore:     0.75,\n\t\tTimestamp:           time.Now(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// checkFresnelBlockageHeuristic uses blob confidence data when no feedback is available\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) checkFresnelBlockageHeuristic(linkID string, history []LinkHealthSnapshot) *Diagnosis {\n\t// Heuristic: look for consistent low composite scores during activity periods\n\t// This would require additional data from the fusion/localization engine\n\t// For now, we'll check if there's consistently degraded health during non-quiet periods\n\n\tvar activityScores []float64\n\tvar quietScores []float64\n\n\tfor _, h := range history {\n\t\tif h.IsQuietPeriod {\n\t\t\tquietScores = append(quietScores, h.CompositeScore)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tactivityScores = append(activityScores, h.CompositeScore)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Need enough activity period data\n\tif len(activityScores) < 10 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate average scores\n\tavgActivity := average(activityScores)\n\tavgQuiet := average(quietScores)\n\n\t// If activity scores are significantly worse than quiet scores,\n\t// there may be detection issues during movement\n\tif avgActivity < 0.5 && avgActivity < avgQuiet-0.2 {\n\t\tnodeBMAC := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n\t\t\tLinkID:           linkID,\n\t\t\tRuleID:           \"fresnel_blockage_heuristic\",\n\t\t\tSeverity:         SeverityWARNING,\n\t\t\tTitle:            \"Possible coverage gap detected\",\n\t\t\tDetail:           \"Detection quality degrades during movement periods. This may indicate an obstruction in the sensing zone.\",\n\t\t\tAdvice:           \"Submit feedback using the app when detection misses occur. This will help identify the exact location of the coverage gap.\",\n\t\t\tRepositioningTarget: nil,\n\t\t\tRepositioningNodeMAC: nodeBMAC,\n\t\t\tConfidenceScore:  0.60, // Lower confidence for heuristic\n\t\t\tTimestamp:        time.Now(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Rule 5: Periodic Interference Spikes\n// Trigger: Periodic spikes in deltaRMS variance (3-10 events per hour, each lasting 1-3 minutes)\n// not correlated with occupancy data.\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) checkPeriodicInterference(linkID string, history []LinkHealthSnapshot) *Diagnosis {\n\t// Look for periodic variance spikes\n\tminEvents := 3\n\tmaxEvents := 10\n\n\t// Find variance spikes\n\tspikes := findVarianceSpikes(history, 2.0) // 2x normal variance\n\n\tif len(spikes) < minEvents || len(spikes) > maxEvents*3 { // Allow some tolerance\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for periodicity (events occur every 6-20 minutes for 3-10 per hour)\n\tif !isPeriodic(spikes, 1*time.Minute, 20*time.Minute) {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate events per hour (handles both ascending and descending order)\n\tstartTime := history[0].Timestamp\n\tendTime := history[len(history)-1].Timestamp\n\thistoryDuration := endTime.Sub(startTime)\n\tif historyDuration < 0 {\n\t\thistoryDuration = -historyDuration\n\t}\n\n\tif historyDuration < time.Hour {\n\t\treturn nil // Need at least an hour of data\n\t}\n\n\teventsPerHour := float64(len(spikes)) / historyDuration.Hours()\n\tif eventsPerHour < float64(minEvents) || eventsPerHour > float64(maxEvents) {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tnodeAMAC := extractNodeAMAC(linkID)\n\tnodeBMAC := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n\n\treturn &Diagnosis{\n\t\tLinkID:           linkID,\n\t\tRuleID:           \"periodic_interference\",\n\t\tSeverity:         SeverityWARNING,\n\t\tTitle:            \"Periodic interference detected\",\n\t\tDetail:           formatInterferenceDetail(nodeAMAC, nodeBMAC, int(eventsPerHour)),\n\t\tAdvice:           formatInterferenceAdvice(nodeAMAC, nodeBMAC),\n\t\tRepositioningTarget: nil,\n\t\tConfidenceScore:  0.70,\n\t\tTimestamp:        time.Now(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// storeDiagnoses stores recent diagnoses for a link\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) storeDiagnoses(linkID string, diagnoses []Diagnosis) {\n\tde.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer de.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tde.recentDiagnoses[linkID] = append(diagnoses, de.recentDiagnoses[linkID]...)\n\tif len(de.recentDiagnoses[linkID]) > de.maxDiagnoses {\n\t\tde.recentDiagnoses[linkID] = de.recentDiagnoses[linkID][:de.maxDiagnoses]\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetDiagnoses returns recent diagnoses for a link\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) GetDiagnoses(linkID string) []Diagnosis {\n\tde.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer de.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tdiagnoses := de.recentDiagnoses[linkID]\n\tresult := make([]Diagnosis, len(diagnoses))\n\tcopy(result, diagnoses)\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// GetAllDiagnoses returns all recent diagnoses\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) GetAllDiagnoses() map[string][]Diagnosis {\n\tde.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer de.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tresult := make(map[string][]Diagnosis)\n\tfor linkID, diagnoses := range de.recentDiagnoses {\n\t\tresult[linkID] = make([]Diagnosis, len(diagnoses))\n\t\tcopy(result[linkID], diagnoses)\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// RunDiagnosticPass triggers an immediate diagnostic pass (for testing)\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) RunDiagnosticPass() {\n\tde.runDiagnosticPass()\n}\n\n// Helper functions\n\nfunc calculateAverageDrift(history []LinkHealthSnapshot) float64 {\n\tif len(history) == 0 {\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n\tvar sum float64\n\tfor _, h := range history {\n\t\tsum += h.DriftRate\n\t}\n\treturn sum / float64(len(history))\n}\n\nfunc average(values []float64) float64 {\n\tif len(values) == 0 {\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n\tvar sum float64\n\tfor _, v := range values {\n\t\tsum += v\n\t}\n\treturn sum / float64(len(values))\n}\n\nfunc extractNodeAMAC(linkID string) string {\n\tif len(linkID) < 17 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\treturn linkID[:17]\n}\n\nfunc extractNodeBMAC(linkID string) string {\n\tif len(linkID) < 35 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\treturn linkID[18:]\n}\n\nfunc findClusterCenter(events []FeedbackEvent) Vec3 {\n\tif len(events) == 0 {\n\t\treturn Vec3{}\n\t}\n\n\tvar sumX, sumY, sumZ float64\n\tfor _, e := range events {\n\t\tsumX += e.Position.X\n\t\tsumY += e.Position.Y\n\t\tsumZ += e.Position.Z\n\t}\n\tn := float64(len(events))\n\treturn Vec3{X: sumX / n, Y: sumY / n, Z: sumZ / n}\n}\n\nfunc findVarianceSpikes(history []LinkHealthSnapshot, threshold float64) []time.Time {\n\tif len(history) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate baseline variance\n\tvar sum float64\n\tfor _, h := range history {\n\t\tsum += h.DeltaRMSVariance\n\t}\n\tbaseline := sum / float64(len(history))\n\n\tif baseline == 0 {\n\t\tbaseline = 1 // Avoid division by zero\n\t}\n\n\t// First, identify all high-variance samples with their timestamps\n\ttype spikeSample struct {\n\t\tts       time.Time\n\t\tvariance float64\n\t}\n\tvar allSpikes []spikeSample\n\tfor _, h := range history {\n\t\tif h.DeltaRMSVariance > baseline*threshold {\n\t\t\tallSpikes = append(allSpikes, spikeSample{ts: h.Timestamp, variance: h.DeltaRMSVariance})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif len(allSpikes) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort spikes by timestamp\n\tsort.Slice(allSpikes, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\treturn allSpikes[i].ts.Before(allSpikes[j].ts)\n\t})\n\n\t// Cluster consecutive spikes (within 2 minutes) into events\n\t// Return the start time of each event\n\tvar events []time.Time\n\teventStart := allSpikes[0].ts\n\tlastSpikeTime := allSpikes[0].ts\n\n\tfor i := 1; i < len(allSpikes); i++ {\n\t\t// If this spike is more than 2 minutes after the last, it's a new event\n\t\tif allSpikes[i].ts.Sub(lastSpikeTime) > 2*time.Minute {\n\t\t\tevents = append(events, eventStart)\n\t\t\teventStart = allSpikes[i].ts\n\t\t}\n\t\tlastSpikeTime = allSpikes[i].ts\n\t}\n\t// Add the last event\n\tevents = append(events, eventStart)\n\n\treturn events\n}\n\nfunc isPeriodic(spikes []time.Time, minInterval, maxInterval time.Duration) bool {\n\tif len(spikes) < 3 {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort spikes by timestamp to handle any order\n\tsortedSpikes := make([]time.Time, len(spikes))\n\tcopy(sortedSpikes, spikes)\n\tsort.Slice(sortedSpikes, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\treturn sortedSpikes[i].Before(sortedSpikes[j])\n\t})\n\n\t// Calculate intervals between spikes\n\tintervals := make([]time.Duration, len(sortedSpikes)-1)\n\tfor i := 0; i < len(sortedSpikes)-1; i++ {\n\t\tintervals[i] = sortedSpikes[i+1].Sub(sortedSpikes[i])\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if intervals are relatively consistent (within 50% of each other)\n\tif len(intervals) == 0 {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tavgInterval := averageDurations(intervals)\n\tif avgInterval < minInterval || avgInterval > maxInterval*3 {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Count intervals within tolerance of average\n\ttolerance := 0.5\n\twithinTolerance := 0\n\tfor _, interval := range intervals {\n\t\tratio := float64(interval) / float64(avgInterval)\n\t\tif ratio > (1-tolerance) && ratio < (1+tolerance) {\n\t\t\twithinTolerance++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// At least 60% of intervals should be within tolerance\n\treturn float64(withinTolerance)/float64(len(intervals)) >= 0.6\n}\n\nfunc averageDurations(durations []time.Duration) time.Duration {\n\tif len(durations) == 0 {\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n\tvar sum time.Duration\n\tfor _, d := range durations {\n\t\tsum += d\n\t}\n\treturn sum / time.Duration(len(durations))\n}\n\n// Formatting helpers\n\nfunc formatWiFiDetail(nodeMAC string, avgPacketRate float64) string {\n\tpct := int(avgPacketRate / 20.0 * 100)\n\tif nodeMAC != \"\" {\n\t\treturn formatNodeLabel(nodeMAC) + \" is only delivering \" + formatInt(pct) + \"% of the expected 20 packets per second. The most common causes are distance from the WiFi router or congestion from nearby networks.\"\n\t}\n\treturn \"This link is only delivering \" + formatInt(pct) + \"% of the expected packets. The most common causes are distance from the WiFi router or congestion from nearby networks.\"\n}\n\nfunc formatWiFiAdvice(nodeMAC string) string {\n\tlabel := \"the node\"\n\tif nodeMAC != \"\" {\n\t\tlabel = formatNodeLabel(nodeMAC)\n\t}\n\treturn \"1. Move \" + label + \" within 10 metres of your WiFi router. \" +\n\t\t\"2. If already close, check if the 2.4GHz channel is congested (3+ networks on overlapping channels). \" +\n\t\t\"3. ESP32-S3 supports both 2.4GHz and 5GHz - if your router supports 5GHz, update the node's WiFi config to use the 5GHz SSID.\"\n}\n\nfunc formatMetalTitle(nodeMAC string) string {\n\tif nodeMAC != \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"Metal interference near \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeMAC)\n\t}\n\treturn \"Metal interference detected\"\n}\n\nfunc formatMetalDetail(linkID string) string {\n\tnodeA := extractNodeAMAC(linkID)\n\tnodeB := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n\tif nodeA != \"\" && nodeB != \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"The sensing link \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeA) + \" to \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeB) + \" has unstable phase measurements even when no one is moving. This is typically caused by metal objects in the near field of the node's antenna (within 10cm): metal shelves, radiators, TV backs, or large appliances.\"\n\t}\n\treturn \"This sensing link has unstable phase measurements even when no one is moving. This is typically caused by metal objects in the near field of the node's antenna (within 10cm).\"\n}\n\nfunc formatMetalAdvice(nodeMAC string) string {\n\tlabel := \"the node\"\n\tif nodeMAC != \"\" {\n\t\tlabel = formatNodeLabel(nodeMAC)\n\t}\n\treturn \"Check for metal objects within 10cm of \" + label + \". \" +\n\t\t\"If \" + label + \" is on a metal surface or shelf, mount it on a non-metal bracket or wall. \" +\n\t\t\"Try repositioning it 20-30cm away from metal surfaces.\"\n}\n\nfunc formatFresnelDetail(linkID, zoneDesc string) string {\n\tnodeA := extractNodeAMAC(linkID)\n\tnodeB := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n\tif nodeA != \"\" && nodeB != \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"The area near \" + zoneDesc + \" shows lower detection coverage. An obstacle may be blocking the path between \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeA) + \" and \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeB) + \", interrupting their sensing zone.\"\n\t}\n\treturn \"The area near \" + zoneDesc + \" shows lower detection coverage. An obstacle may be blocking the sensing zone.\"\n}\n\nfunc formatFresnelAdvice(nodeMAC string, blockedZone, target *Vec3, improvement float64) string {\n\tif target == nil || nodeMAC == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"Check for obstructions between the nodes. Large furniture, appliances, or metal objects can block the sensing zone.\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Calculate direction from current position\n\tdirection := calculateDirection(blockedZone, target)\n\tdistance := calculateDistance(blockedZone, target)\n\n\tadvice := \"Move \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeMAC) + \" \" + direction + \" by approximately \" + formatDistance(distance) + \" to restore coverage.\"\n\n\tif improvement > 0 {\n\t\tadvice += \" The target position is marked in green in the 3D view.\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn advice\n}\n\nfunc formatInterferenceDetail(nodeA, nodeB string, eventsPerHour int) string {\n\tif nodeA != \"\" && nodeB != \"\" {\n\t\treturn formatNodeLabel(nodeA) + \" to \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeB) + \" is experiencing regular interference bursts \" + formatInt(eventsPerHour) + \" times per hour. This pattern is consistent with a microwave oven, a cordless phone, or a pulsed 2.4GHz source.\"\n\t}\n\treturn \"This link is experiencing regular interference bursts \" + formatInt(eventsPerHour) + \" times per hour. This pattern is consistent with a microwave oven, a cordless phone, or a pulsed 2.4GHz source.\"\n}\n\nfunc formatInterferenceAdvice(nodeA, nodeB string) string {\n\tnodes := \"the nodes\"\n\tif nodeA != \"\" && nodeB != \"\" {\n\t\tnodes = formatNodeLabel(nodeA) + \" or \" + formatNodeLabel(nodeB)\n\t}\n\treturn \"Consider the following: \" +\n\t\t\"1. Is \" + nodes + \" near a kitchen? Microwave ovens cause strong 2.4GHz interference. \" +\n\t\t\"2. A cordless DECT phone or baby monitor near one of the nodes may be the source. \" +\n\t\t\"3. Try moving the affected node at least 2 metres from any 2.4GHz appliances.\"\n}\n\nfunc formatZoneDescription(pos Vec3) string {\n\t// Simple zone description based on position\n\t// This could be enhanced with room-relative positioning\n\treturn formatFloat(pos.X, 1) + \"m, \" + formatFloat(pos.Z, 1) + \"m from origin\"\n}\n\nfunc formatNodeLabel(mac string) string {\n\tif len(mac) >= 8 {\n\t\treturn \"Node \" + mac[:8]\n\t}\n\treturn \"Node\"\n}\n\nfunc calculateDirection(from, to *Vec3) string {\n\tif from == nil || to == nil {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tdx := to.X - from.X\n\tdz := to.Z - from.Z\n\n\tvar direction string\n\tif math.Abs(dz) > math.Abs(dx) {\n\t\tif dz > 0 {\n\t\t\tdirection = \"forward\"\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tdirection = \"backward\"\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tif dx > 0 {\n\t\t\tdirection = \"right\"\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tdirection = \"left\"\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn direction\n}\n\nfunc calculateDistance(from, to *Vec3) float64 {\n\tif from == nil || to == nil {\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n\tdx := to.X - from.X\n\tdz := to.Z - from.Z\n\treturn math.Sqrt(dx*dx + dz*dz)\n}\n\nfunc formatDistance(m float64) string {\n\tif m < 0.5 {\n\t\treturn formatFloat(m*100, 0) + \"cm\"\n\t}\n\treturn formatFloat(m, 1) + \" metres\"\n}\n\nfunc formatInt(n int) string {\n\tif n < 0 {\n\t\treturn \"0\"\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", n)\n}\n\nfunc formatFloat(f float64, decimals int) string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%.[2]*[1]f\", f, decimals)\n}\n\n// Tooltip represents a contextual help tooltip for a feature.\ntype Tooltip struct {\n\tTitle       string\n\tDescription string\n\tDirection   string // \"top\", \"bottom\", \"left\", \"right\"\n}\n\n// GetDiagnosticFor returns diagnostic information for a specific link at a given timestamp.\n// This is used by the post-feedback explanation system to provide root cause analysis\n// for false positive detections.\nfunc (de *DiagnosticEngine) GetDiagnosticFor(linkID string, timestamp time.Time) *Diagnosis {\n\tde.mu.RLock()\n\tgetHealthHistory := de.getHealthHistory\n\twindow := de.config.HistoryWindow\n\tde.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tif getHealthHistory == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Get health history around the specified timestamp\n\thistory := getHealthHistory(linkID, window)\n\tif len(history) < de.config.MinSamples {\n\t\t// Not enough data - return a generic diagnosis\n\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n\t\t\tLinkID:           linkID,\n\t\t\tRuleID:           \"insufficient_data\",\n\t\t\tSeverity:         SeverityINFO,\n\t\t\tTitle:            \"Not enough data for diagnosis\",\n\t\t\tDetail:           \"This link hasn't been active long enough to analyze.\",\n\t\t\tAdvice:           \"Continue normal operation. Diagnostics will be available after more data is collected.\",\n\t\t\tConfidenceScore:  0.0,\n\t\t\tTimestamp:        time.Now(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Find the snapshot closest to the requested timestamp\n\tvar closestSnapshot *LinkHealthSnapshot\n\tminDiff := time.Duration(1<<63 - 1) // Max duration\n\n\tfor i := range history {\n\t\tdiff := history[i].Timestamp.Sub(timestamp)\n\t\tif diff < 0 {\n\t\t\tdiff = -diff\n\t\t}\n\t\tif diff < minDiff {\n\t\t\tminDiff = diff\n\t\t\tclosestSnapshot = &history[i]\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif closestSnapshot == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Check which diagnostic rules would have fired at that time\n\t// We'll run a focused diagnostic check around the timestamp\n\n\t// Rule: Check for environmental change indicators\n\tif closestSnapshot.DriftRate > 0.05 {\n\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n\t\t\tLinkID:   linkID,\n\t\t\tRuleID:   \"environmental_change\",\n\t\t\tSeverity: SeverityINFO,\n\t\t\tTitle:    \"Possible environmental change\",\n\t\t\tDetail:   \"The baseline for this link has drifted significantly. This could be caused by temperature changes, furniture movement, or new obstructions.\",\n\t\t\tAdvice:   \"The system is adapting automatically. No action needed unless this persists.\",\n\t\t\tConfidenceScore: 0.70,\n\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Rule: Check for WiFi congestion indicators\n\tif closestSnapshot.PacketRate < 16.0 { // Less than 80% of expected 20 Hz\n\t\tnodeBMAC := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n\t\t\tLinkID:   linkID,\n\t\t\tRuleID:   \"wifi_congestion\",\n\t\t\tSeverity: SeverityACTIONABLE,\n\t\t\tTitle:    \"Possible WiFi congestion\",\n\t\t\tDetail:   formatWiFiDetail(nodeBMAC, closestSnapshot.PacketRate),\n\t\t\tAdvice:   formatWiFiAdvice(nodeBMAC),\n\t\t\tRepositioningNodeMAC: nodeBMAC,\n\t\t\tConfidenceScore: 0.75,\n\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Rule: Check for phase instability (metal interference)\n\tif closestSnapshot.PhaseStability > 0.6 {\n\t\tnodeAMAC := extractNodeAMAC(linkID)\n\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n\t\t\tLinkID:   linkID,\n\t\t\tRuleID:   \"metal_interference\",\n\t\t\tSeverity: SeverityACTIONABLE,\n\t\t\tTitle:    formatMetalTitle(nodeAMAC),\n\t\t\tDetail:   formatMetalDetail(linkID),\n\t\t\tAdvice:   formatMetalAdvice(nodeAMAC),\n\t\t\tRepositioningNodeMAC: nodeAMAC,\n\t\t\tConfidenceScore: 0.80,\n\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Rule: Check for periodic interference patterns\n\tif closestSnapshot.DeltaRMSVariance > 2.0 {\n\t\tnodeAMAC := extractNodeAMAC(linkID)\n\t\tnodeBMAC := extractNodeBMAC(linkID)\n\t\treturn &Diagnosis{\n\t\t\tLinkID:   linkID,\n\t\t\tRuleID:   \"periodic_interference\",\n\t\t\tSeverity: SeverityWARNING,\n\t\t\tTitle:    \"Possible periodic interference\",\n\t\t\tDetail:   formatInterferenceDetail(nodeAMAC, nodeBMAC, 3),\n\t\t\tAdvice:   formatInterferenceAdvice(nodeAMAC, nodeBMAC),\n\t\t\tConfidenceScore: 0.70,\n\t\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Default: no specific issue found\n\treturn &Diagnosis{\n\t\tLinkID:   linkID,\n\t\tRuleID:   \"no_issue_detected\",\n\t\tSeverity: SeverityINFO,\n\t\tTitle:    \"No specific issue detected\",\n\t\tDetail:   \"The link health metrics appear normal. The false positive may be due to transient RF interference or a brief environmental change.\",\n\t\tAdvice:   \"This is a rare occurrence. If it happens frequently, consider submitting feedback so the system can learn.\",\n\t\tConfidenceScore: 0.50,\n\t\tTimestamp: time.Now(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetDiagnosisExplanation returns a plain-language explanation for a diagnosis.\nfunc (d *Diagnosis) GetDiagnosisExplanation() string {\n\tif d == nil {\n\t\treturn \"No diagnostic information available.\"\n\t}\n\n\texplanation := d.Detail\n\n\t// Add context-specific advice\n\tif d.RuleID == \"environmental_change\" {\n\t\texplanation += \" The system adapts to these changes automatically over time.\"\n\t} else if d.RuleID == \"wifi_congestion\" {\n\t\texplanation += \" Moving the node closer to your WiFi router may help.\"\n\t} else if d.RuleID == \"metal_interference\" {\n\t\texplanation += \" Check for metal objects within 10cm of the node antenna.\"\n\t} else if d.RuleID == \"periodic_interference\" {\n\t\texplanation += \" This pattern often indicates a microwave oven or other 2.4GHz device.\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn explanation\n}\n","numLines":1168,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1168}}}
